<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">&lt;!-- This example adapted from the PET Handbook, copyright University of Cambridge ESOL Examinations --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">&lt;!-- The example combines shuffle with the fixation of one simpleChoice in place --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">&lt;!-- Ruby markup is defined in NS hq5 (HTML5 for QTI), which is referenced in the main QTI 2.2 XSD --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">m:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot; http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0 </span></span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd </span></span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/mathml/v3p0/schema/xsd/mathml3.xsd&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;choice_ruby&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;出身地&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">adaptive=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span> <span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;ja&quot;</span> &gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;ChoiceHK&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;0&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;村田&lt;<span class="kw">ruby</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">rb</span>&gt;真&lt;/<span class="kw">rb</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">rt</span>&gt;まこと&lt;/<span class="kw">rt</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">ruby</span>&gt;の出身地はとこてすか&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">max-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-labels-cjk-ideographic&quot;</span> &gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;選ひなさい&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceHK&quot;</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">ruby</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">rb</span>&gt;北海道&lt;/<span class="kw">rb</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">rt</span>&gt;ほっかいとう&lt;/<span class="kw">rt</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">ruby</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceTH&quot;</span>&gt;東北&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceHR&quot;</span>&gt;北陸&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceKT&quot;</span>&gt;関東&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceKST&quot;</span>&gt;甲信越&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceKK&quot;</span>&gt;近畿&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-34"><a href="#cb1-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceKS&quot;</span>&gt;関西&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-35"><a href="#cb1-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceSK&quot;</span>&gt;四国&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-36"><a href="#cb1-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceTC&quot;</span>&gt;中国&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-37"><a href="#cb1-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceKY&quot;</span>&gt;九州&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-38"><a href="#cb1-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-39"><a href="#cb1-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-40"><a href="#cb1-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-41"><a href="#cb1-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   <span class="ot">template=</span><span class="st">&quot;https://www.imsglobal.org/question/qti_v3p0/rptemplates/match_correct.xml&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-42"><a href="#cb1-42" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><figure id="fig-hometown-japanese">
<img
src="/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl2_ruby_default.png"
width="360"
alt="A sample choiceInteraction item using Ruby Markup in Japanese characters." />
<figcaption>Figure 4 <span class="fig-title">Ruby Example (Japanese) -
default Choice Interaction rendering</span></figcaption>
</figure></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

In this next ruby example, the standardized shared interaction
vocabulary is used to show the text vertically from right to left. Go to
the [QTI 3 Standardized Shared Vocabulary and CSS Classes
document](https://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/vocab) for additional
vertical text options.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">&lt;!-- This example adapted from the PET Handbook, copyright University of Cambridge ESOL Examinations --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot; http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0 </span></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;choice_ruby_i23n&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;出身地&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">adaptive=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span> <span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;ja&quot;</span> &gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;ChoiceHK&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;0&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">div</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-height-96 qti-writing-mode-vertical-rl qti-float-right&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;村田&lt;<span class="kw">ruby</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">rb</span>&gt;真&lt;/<span class="kw">rb</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">rt</span>&gt;まこと&lt;/<span class="kw">rt</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">ruby</span>&gt;の出身地はとこてすか&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">max-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;1&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-labels-cjk-ideographic qti-writing-orientation-vertical-lr&quot;</span> &gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;選ひなさい&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceHK&quot;</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">ruby</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">rb</span>&gt;北海道&lt;/<span class="kw">rb</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">rt</span>&gt;ほっかいとう&lt;/<span class="kw">rt</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">ruby</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceTH&quot;</span>&gt;東北&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceHR&quot;</span>&gt;北陸&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceKT&quot;</span>&gt;関東&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceKST&quot;</span>&gt;甲信越&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceKK&quot;</span>&gt;近畿&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceKS&quot;</span>&gt;関西&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceSK&quot;</span>&gt;四国&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-34"><a href="#cb1-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceTC&quot;</span>&gt;中国&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-35"><a href="#cb1-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceKY&quot;</span>&gt;九州&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-36"><a href="#cb1-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-37"><a href="#cb1-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-38"><a href="#cb1-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-39"><a href="#cb1-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-40"><a href="#cb1-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   <span class="ot">template=</span><span class="st">&quot;https://www.imsglobal.org/question/qti_v3p0/rptemplates/match_correct.xml&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-41"><a href="#cb1-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><figure id="fig-hometown-japanese-with-vertical-text-right-to-left">
<img src="/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl2_ruby_i23n.png"
width="800"
alt="A sample choiceInteraction item using Ruby Markup in Japanese characters." />
<figcaption>Figure 5 <span class="fig-title">Ruby Example with Vertical
Text, set Right-to-Left (Japanese)</span></figcaption>
</figure></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div id="item-structure" class="section">

## 3. Item Structure<a href="#h.77l136x98r3" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.77l136x98r3" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Items are the essential building blocks of an assessment and make up the
bulk of the test material. Items contain the assessment stimuli and
prompts (the questions), and also the response areas (the
interactions) and scoring instructions.

In order for organizations to exchange the assessment items, the item
files may also need to contain a variety of data elements that support
the item content. These data elements  are briefly explained in this
subsection, and with greater details throughout Section 3 of this
implementation guide. An assessment item encompasses the information
that is presented to a candidate and information about how to score the
item. Scoring takes place when candidate responses are transformed into
outcomes by response processing rules.

<div id="the-qti-assessment-item-element" class="section">

### 3.1 The qti-assessment-item Element<a href="#h.dltnnj87l0yj" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.dltnnj87l0yj" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The qti-assessment-item element contains all the other QTI 3 item
structures, and the opening qti-assessment-item element contains the
following attributes (\* denotes the attribute is required):

- All namespace declarations, including schema locations\*
- identifier\*
- title\*
- label
- language (xml:lang)
- tool name (tool-name)
- tool version (tool-version)
- adaptive item indicator (adaptive)
- time dependent (time-dependent) item indicator\*

The qti-assessment-item node must contain an "identifier" attribute with
a non-null string to identify the item. It is used to identify the item
as a unique entity within the context of the exchange of assessment
content, so organizations may wish to add organizational prefixes to
their identifier information to ensure uniqueness among multiple
vendors.

The "title" is an additional required attribute, which generally
contains a more human readable string of text that describes the item.
It is often presented to authors and reviewers, sometimes to candidates.

You can optionally add a "label" as an attribute, in whatever way is
meaningful as an organizational label for the item(s). The 'label'
attribute is typically used to identify, or label, workflow or process
aspects of the Item. Typically the value in 'label' is used to support
various tools.

The "xml:lang" attribute is optional, though it should be included as a
best practice, as the language is a primary accessibility support for
web-based documents.

Both the "tool-name" and "tool-version" are optional elements, which
usually indicate the tool name and version that generated the item.

The "adaptive" attribute is also optional, and the default value is
false, where only boolean values (true/false) are permitted. By not
including the adaptive attribute, the item will NOT be considered
adaptive (changing to the responses of the candidate). The adaptive
attribute is not related to adaptive assessments, where the sequence of
the item within the context of an assessment is non-linear. See [Section
3.7.2](#h.pd5mfepf6mk9)  for more information on item adaptivity.

The "time-dependent" attribute is a required attribute, where boolean
values (true/false) are permitted. A time dependent item
(time-dependent="true") is one in which time; i.e., the amount of time a
candidate is provided by a delivery system to enter a response, is
important for the psychometric properties - the calibration - of the
item. Along with response evaluation, this attribute can also be used in
conjunction with other Test attributes by delivery systems to enforce
candidate attempt duration limits.

<div id="structures-within-the-qti-assessment-item-node"
class="section">

#### 3.1.1 Structures within the qti-assessment-item Node<a href="#h.jw04arywuimn" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.jw04arywuimn" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The QTI-specific XML structures are described in the QTI 3Item XSD. The
QTI 3 Item XSD can be found at
<https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_itemv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd>
, which is a subset of the full QTI 3 XSD found at
https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd.
The namespace is http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0

An item typically includes the following information:

- Scoring data
- The item content presented to the candidate, which itself contains:

<!-- -->

- Any directions/instructions
- Any stimuli or references to stimuli
- The prompt(s)
- The interaction(s)
- Feedback for the candidate
- Supplemental content or references to supplemental content
- Content displayed, voiced, or printed, contingent on the candidate's
  Personal Needs and Preferences (PNP)

The above information is stored in specific QTI 3structures, and must be
supplied in the sequence listed below :

1.  qti-context-declaration
2.  qti-response-declaration
3.  qti-outcome-declaration
4.  qti-template-declaration
5.  qti-template-processing
6.  qti-assessment-stimulus-ref
7.  qti-companion-materials-info
8.  qti-response-processing
9.  qti-modal-feedback

The inclusion or exclusion of the above structures depends on the
complexity of the item, and on the needs of the assessment program using
the assessment content.

Scoring an item can be quite complex, and the information used to
provide the response information and the score(s)received (the
outcome(s)) are possible using a combination of the following
structures:

1.  qit-context-declaration
2.  qti-response-declaration
3.  qti-outcome-declaration
4.  qti-response-processing

Content (and content presentation data) to be presented to the candidate
is supplied or referenced in a combination of these structures:

1.  qti-assessment-stimulus-ref
2.  qti-companion-materials-info
3.  qti-stylesheet
4.  qti-item-body

Content presented to candidates using variables use these structures:

1.  qti-template-declaration
2.  qti-template-processing
3.  qti-catalog-info
4.  qti-modal-feedback

A brief introduction to the AssessmentItem structures is provided below.

<div id="context-declaration" class="section">

##### 3.1.1.1 Context Declaration<a href="#h.1apkhct4984m" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.1apkhct4984m" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The qti-context-declaration nodes are used to make "contextual" variable
declarations that are global in scope to an item. When evaluating
TemplateProcessing and ResponseProcessing instructions in an
AssessmentItem, having contextual information available in the
information model can improve the effectiveness, efficiency, and
personalization of TemplateProcessing and ResponseProcessing
instructions. Contextual information can include candidate information,
test information, and system (or environmental) information. Context
declarations are also permitted in tests.  See [Section
3.7.11](#h.7tgz0gr02037)  for a longer description of context
declarations.

</div>

<div id="response-declaration" class="section">

##### 3.1.1.2 Response Declaration<a href="#h.wq4e8lbs4wa9" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.wq4e8lbs4wa9" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Response variables are declared using qti-response-declaration nodes.
Interactions in the itemBody must be bound to one of these declared
variables . The response declaration supplies the values for the scoring
of the item. It may indicate what the correct response is, or the
partially correct response(s). The base-type and cardinality defined for
a response variable must match the types and cardinality required by the
interactions bound to that response variable.

</div>

<div id="outcome-declaration" class="section">

##### 3.1.1.3 Outcome Declaration<a href="#h.8eeljboxridc" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.8eeljboxridc" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Outcome variables are declared using qti-outcome-declaration nodes. The
outcome variables represent the score that the candidate can receive for
responding to, or not responding to, the item. The outcomes variables
values are set either from a default given in the outcome declaration
itself or by a ResponseRule during ResponseProcessing. See [Section
3.5](#h.886q73e2ya9q)  for a longer description of outcome declarations.

</div>

<div id="template-declaration" class="section">

##### 3.1.1.4 Template Declaration<a href="#h.s6zmzsnp3ewc" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.s6zmzsnp3ewc" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Use qti-template-declaration nodes to declare item variables that are to
be used specifically for the purposes of cloning items. Template
variables can have their value set only during TemplateProcessing.
Template declarations are referred to within the qti-item-body in order
to individualize the clone and possibly also within the
ResponseProcessing rules if the cloning process affects the way the item
is scored. See [Section 3.7.4](#h.hs6z9wmqtzq7)  for a longer
description of using templates in items.

</div>

<div id="template-processing" class="section">

##### 3.1.1.5 Template Processing<a href="#h.kxzdpny6ki0c" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.kxzdpny6ki0c" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Use the qti-template-processing node for template processing. Template
processing consists of one or more TemplateRules that are followed by a
cloning engine or delivery system in order to assign values to the
template variables. Template processing is identical in form to
ResponseProcessing except that the purpose is to assign values to
template variables, not outcome variables. See [Section
3.7.4](#h.hs6z9wmqtzq7)  for a description on using template processing
in items.

##### Reference to a Shared Stimulus<a href="#h.kxzdpny6ki0d" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.kxzdpny6ki0d" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The qti-assessment-stimulus-ref element can be used to associate one or
more external; i.e., external to the qti-assessment-item XML, stimulus
blocks with a particular item.  A common use case is to author and
package a stimulus such as a passage, which can then be shared among
multiple items. See [Section 3.7.7](#h.pb0clew9cko9)  for more specifics
on how to link items to a shared stimulus.

</div>

<div id="companion-materials" class="section">

##### 3.1.1.6 Companion Materials<a href="#h.n5l4l6ifzrdo" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.n5l4l6ifzrdo" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The qti-companion-materials-info node is used to indicate if the item
should be presented to candidates with other kinds of (built-in)
assessment tools or materials (companion materials), including:
calculators, protractors, rulers, digital materials, or physical
materials. See [Section 3.7.8](#h.cog67s90rmps)  for a longer
description of including companion materials in items.

</div>

<div id="stylesheets" class="section">

##### 3.1.1.7 Stylesheets<a href="#h.5nfxp42xsdds" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.5nfxp42xsdds" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The qti-stylesheet nodes are used to associate external stylesheets for
the item. QTI 3 supports CSS 2.1 and CSS 3.0 stylesheets (excluding
speech). Any number of stylesheets can be included in an item. See
[Section 3.6](#h.fu8olo1248hn)  for examples of how to include
stylesheets in items.

For conformance purposes, the use of stylesheets may be ignored by
delivery systems, where support for stylesheets is not required for QTI
applications.

For interoperability and implementation purposes, QTI shared interaction
vocabulary and CSS classes take precedence over CSS stylesheets. See the
[QTI 3 Standardized Shared Vocabulary and CSS
Classes](https://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/vocab) document for
full details.

</div>

<div id="the-item-body" class="section">

##### 3.1.1.8 The Item Body<a href="#h.sphpo6lu6zqi" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.sphpo6lu6zqi" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The qti-item-body node contains the text, graphics, media objects and
interactions that describe the item's content and information about how
it is structured. Only one qti-item-body node is allowed within an item.
The qti-item-body is presented to candidates by combining the
qti-item-body content with stylesheet information, either explicitly or
implicitly using the default style rules of the delivery or authoring
system. The ItemBody contains formatted, structured HTML content (see
@@@ [Section 2.3]()), as well as any prompt content (optionally
contained within the qti-prompt nodes) and any interactions.

Composite items are items that contain more than one interaction within
the ItemBody. Composite items may contain multiple instances of the same
type of interaction or have a mixture of interaction types within the
ItemBody. See [Section 3.2](#h.7jm6la7pne1i)  for documentation for
specific QTI interactions, and [Section 3.3](#h.fzolwwxsh7ga)  for the
use of composite items. Note that while the qti-item-body node may
contain multiple interactions, any interaction *MUST NOT* be nested or
contained within other interactions. Items with nested interactions are
not considered compliant to the QTI standard regardless of whether they
pass validation or not.

Because ItemBody content is expected to be presented to candidates with
a wide variance of perceptual, cognitive processing, physical
capabilities, etc.; assessment content should be made as accessible as
possible to the widest range of candidates. Guidance on how to provide
accessible content is sprinkled throughout this implementation guide,
but is certainly not the definitive nor complete documentation on
providing accessible content to people. Implementers should stay current
in methods and approaches that help increase access to assessment
content for all users.

The ItemBody content must be presented to the candidate during an active
assessment session. In the assessment session, the candidate must be
able to interact with each of the presented interactions and therefore
set or update the values of the associated response variables.

The ItemBody may be presented to the candidate when the item session is
in the closed or review state. In these states, although the candidate's
responses should be presented, the interactions must be disabled so as
to prevent the candidate from setting or updating the values of the
associated response variables.

The ItemBody may be presented to the candidate in the solution state, in
which case the correct values of the response variables must be
presented and the associated interactions disabled.

Adaptive items are a feature that allows an item to be scored adaptively
over a sequence of attempts. This allows the candidate to alter their
answer following feedback or to be posed additional questions based on
their current answer.

The ItemBody may also contain rubric blocks (qti-rubric-block), which is
content that may be presented to one of the possible audiences for the
content, including the author, candidate, proctor, scorer,
testConstructor, or tutor. This content would only be shown to the
specified audience when the role of that audience is specifically known
the system presenting the item. See [Section 3.7.5](#h.jr9dryq0pjvh)
 for more information on using rubric blocks in an assessmentItem.

</div>

<div id="catalog-resources" class="section">

##### 3.1.1.9 Catalog Resources<a href="#h.a1ox0r24ofsj" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.a1ox0r24ofsj" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The qti-catalog-info node holds one or more catalogs. The ItemBody
content references specific catalogs within the qti-catalog-info node.
Catalogs hold and reference item content that is presented to candidates
based on their candidate profile (PNP) requirements. See Section 5 for a
longer description of using catalogs to store support-specific content.
 In addition to Section 5, there is an annotated item in [Section
3.8.1](#h.7x7ukes4f72u)  that includes the use of catalogs for encoding
Glossaries and Keyword Translations.

</div>

<div id="response-processing" class="section">

##### 3.1.1.10 Response Processing<a href="#h.14sfq6682qx5" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.14sfq6682qx5" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Use the qti-response-processing node for response processing - the
process by which a delivery engine assigns outcomes (the scores) based
on the candidate's responses. Response processing can be achieved using
either a response processing template (a reference to a common
processing pattern), or by using customized response processing. See
[Section 3.4.2](#h.66wn7ucnbn4v)  for information on using response
processing templates, and [Section 3.7.1](#h.fi29q8dubjgw)  for
information on customized response processing.

</div>

<div id="modal-and-test-feedback" class="section">

##### 3.1.1.11 Modal and Test Feedback<a href="#h.xi9c9k6zajwp" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.xi9c9k6zajwp" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The qti-modal-feedback node is used to provide item-level feedback,
which is presented to the candidate directly following response
processing. See [Section 3.7.3](#h.of39hkegnqll)  for more information
on providing feedback to candidates.  Similarly, qti-test-feedback
provides a method to display feedback at the Assessment Test or Test
Part level and can be presented either during the test or test part
after each instance of outcome processing, or at the conclusion of the
test or test part.

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div id="interaction-types" class="section">

### 3.2 Interaction Types<a href="#h.7jm6la7pne1i" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.7jm6la7pne1i" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

All QTI v2.2 interaction types are included in QTI 3. However, the
interaction elements have been renamed to reflect a web
component-friendly syntax which will enable QTI processors to render
ItemBody interaction content - should they choose to do so - with web
components, and without having to apply further interaction
transformations.

The QTI 3 interaction web components-friendly vocabulary introduces the
"qti-" prefix on every QTI element. Furthermore, camelcase elements and
attributes are replaced with all-lowercase elements/attributes *with
hyphen separators*. See the example below of a choice interaction with
the QTI 3 web components-friendly vocabulary.

**Example: qti-choice-interaction**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  . . .</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Look at the text in the picture.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">img</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;images/sign.png&quot;</span> <span class="ot">alt=</span><span class="st">&quot;NEVER LEAVE LUGGAGE UNATTENDED&quot;</span> /&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span> <span class="ot">max-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;What does it say?&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;A&quot;</span>&gt;You must stay with your luggage at all times.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;B&quot;</span>&gt;Do not let someone else look after your luggage.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;C&quot;</span>&gt;Remember your luggage when you leave.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

In another example (a hotspot interaction), note how the img element is
considered an HTML element - and therefore not prefixed by "qti-" - even
though it is a sub-element of an interaction.

**Example: qti-hotspot-interaction**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  . . .</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;The picture below illustrates four of the most popular destinations for</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    air travelers arriving in the United Kingdom: London, Manchester, Edinburgh and Glasgow.</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    Please choose all of the cities Northof London.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-hotspot-interaction</span> <span class="ot">max-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">img</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;ukair.png&quot;</span> <span class="ot">height=</span><span class="st">&quot;280&quot;</span> <span class="ot">width=</span><span class="st">&quot;206&quot;</span> <span class="ot">alt=</span><span class="st">&quot;UK Map&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-hotspot-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;A&quot;</span> <span class="ot">shape=</span><span class="st">&quot;circle&quot;</span> <span class="ot">coords=</span><span class="st">&quot;77,115,10&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-hotspot-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;B&quot;</span> <span class="ot">shape=</span><span class="st">&quot;circle&quot;</span> <span class="ot">coords=</span><span class="st">&quot;118,184,10&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-hotspot-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;C&quot;</span> <span class="ot">shape=</span><span class="st">&quot;circle&quot;</span> <span class="ot">coords=</span><span class="st">&quot;150,235,10&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-hotspot-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;D&quot;</span> <span class="ot">shape=</span><span class="st">&quot;circle&quot;</span> <span class="ot">coords=</span><span class="st">&quot;96,114,10&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-hotspot-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

QTI 3 includes the following standardized interactions:

1.  **Choice** (qti-choice-interaction): presents a set of choices to
    the candidate. The candidate's task is to select one or more of the
    choices, up to a maximum number of choices allowed. (See [Section
    3.2.2](#h.j9nu1oa1tu3b) )
2.  **Text Entry** (qti-text-entry-interaction): an inline interaction
    that accepts text from the candidate. (See [Section
    3.2.3](#h.5bw8rpbotrcs) )
3.  **Extended Text**(qti-extended-text-interaction): a block
    interaction that allows the candidate to enter an extended amount of
    text.
4.  **Gap Match** (qti-gap-match-interaction): a block interaction that
    contains a number of gaps that the candidate can fill from an
    associated set of choices.
5.  **Hot Spot**(qti-hotspot-interaction): a graphical interaction with
    a corresponding set of choices that are defined as areas of the
    graphic image. The candidate's task is to select one or more of the
    areas (hotspots).
6.  **Hot Text** (qti-hot-text-interaction): presents a set of choices
    to the candidate represented as selectable runs of text embedded
    within a surrounding context, such as a passage of text.
7.  **Inline Choice** (qti-inline-choice-interaction): an inline
    interaction that presents the user with a set of choices, each of
    which is an answer option (usually text). The candidate's task is to
    select one of the choices.
8.  **Match** (qti-match-interaction): a block interaction that presents
    candidates with two sets of choices and allows them to create
    associations between pairs of choices in the two sets, but not
    between pairs of choices in the same set.
9.  **Order** (qti-order-interaction): the candidate's task is to
    reorder the choices, the order in which the choices are displayed
    initially is significant.
10. **Graphic Order**(qti-graphic-order-interaction): A graphic order
    interaction is a graphic interaction with a corresponding set of
    hotspot choices that are defined as areas of the graphic image. The
    candidate's task is to impose an ordering on the areas (hotspots).
11. **Associate** (qti-associate-interaction): a block interaction that
    presents candidates with a number of choices and allows them to
    create associations between them.
12. **Graphic Associate** (qti-graphic-associate-interaction): a graphic
    interaction with a corresponding set of choices that are defined as
    areas of the graphic image. The candidate's task is to associate the
    areas (hotspots) with each other.
13. **Graphic Gap Match** (qti-graphic-gap-match-interaction): a
    graphical interaction with a set of gaps that are defined as areas
    (hotspots) of the graphic image and an additional set of gap choices
    that are defined outside the image. The candidate must associate the
    gap choices with the gaps in the image and be able to review the
    image with the gaps filled in context, as indicated by their
    choices.
14. **Media** (qti-media-interaction): allows more control over the way
    the candidate interacts with a time-based media object and allows
    the number of times the media object was experienced to be reported
    in the value of the associated response variable.
15. **Position Object** (qti-position-object-interaction): consists of a
    single image which must be positioned on another graphic image (the
    stage) by the candidate.
16. **Select Point** (qti-select-point-interaction): a graphic
    interaction in which the candidate's task is to select one or more
    points.
17. **Slider** (qti-slider-interaction): presents the candidate with a
    control for selecting a numerical value between a lower and upper
    bound.
18. **Upload**(qti-upload-interaction): allows the candidate to upload a
    pre-prepared file representing their response.
19. **Drawing**(qti-drawing-interaction): allows the candidate to use
    drawing tools provided by the delivery system to modify a given
    graphical image (the canvas). The result is a file in the same
    format as the original image.
20. **Custom**(qti-custom-interaction) and **Portable
    Custom** (qti-portable-custom-interaction): allow the item author to
    present an interaction with a custom user interface and behavior,
    supported by, respectively, delivery engine-specific or
    author-provided code.  Portable Custom Interactions (PCIs) allow the
    Javascript code implementing the interaction to be made available to
    delivery systems, with a standard Javascript interface, offering the
    potential for making PCIs more interoperable and portable between
    conforming delivery engines.
21. **End Attempt** (qti-end-attempt-interaction): is a special
    interaction which immediately ends the current attempt on an
    assessment item.  It may be used, for example, to allow the
    candidate to request a hint or model solution, or in an adaptive
    item to let the candidate display feedback or to move to the next in
    a series of interactions in the item.

Each of the interactions described in the following sections can contain
sub-elements and attributes. For full descriptions of these features,
see the QTI 3 Information Model document.

<div id="interoperability-and-utilizing-shared-vocabularies"
class="section">

#### 3.2.1 Interoperability and Utilizing Shared Interaction Vocabularies<a href="#h.q0bgy0kk6j43" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.q0bgy0kk6j43" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Previous versions of QTI lack specificity with respect to interaction
presentation, styling, and behavior. Consequently, QTI authoring systems
and delivery systems have implemented interactions with custom
presentation/behavior definitions, which in turn have become an
impediment to interoperability. To improve interaction
presentation/behavior interoperability, QTI 3 introduces, "shared
interaction vocabularies" that describe more fine-grained presentation
control and more semantically-explicit styling and behaviors.

There are two primary mechanisms of the QTI 3 shared interaction
vocabularies:

1.  An interaction's **class** attribute, and
2.  An interaction's " **data-**" attributes.

In order to prevent collisions with existing classes, the QTI 3
interaction shared vocabularies  use classes with the "qti-" prefix.

Although the **class** and " **data-**" attributes already exist in QTI
2.2, the shared interaction vocabularies explicitly define how to use
these attributes to achieve certain agreed-upon rendered presentations.
By agreeing to implement interaction styling and behaviors according to
the definitions of the shared interaction vocabularies, interaction
interoperability may be greatly improved.

Implementers of *custom* presentations/behaviors should avoid using
classes with the "qti-" prefix, and should also avoid using the QTI 3
established "data-" attributes from the shared interaction vocabularies.
Over time, it is the expectation that the shared interaction
vocabularies will evolve as common use-cases emerge.

When using classes that indicate that the presentation of content should
be horizontal (qti-orientation-horizontal, qti-choices-stacking-2,
etc.), it can force some of the choices outside the single width of the
viewing portal, which can be problematic for candidates. For low-vision
candidates using magnification (or text-appearance -> font-size or
word-spacing), the likelihood of horizontal scrolling is increased. When
choices are placed off-screen, candidates can accidentally disregard the
choices, candidates are required to use additional working memory to
respond to the item, and it adds significant time for low-vision
candidates.

If the candidate is known to have low-vision, and choices could be
displayed beyond the width of a single viewport, presentation systems
should consider presenting the choices vertically.

The [1EdTech QTI 3 Standardized Shared Vocabulary and CSS Classes
document](https://www.imsglobal.org/node/218713) enumerates and details
all of the currently adopted shared vocabulary.

</div>

<div id="choice-interaction" class="section">

#### 3.2.2 Choice Interaction<a href="#h.j9nu1oa1tu3b" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.j9nu1oa1tu3b" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The ChoiceInteraction.Type (qti-choice-interaction) interaction presents
a collection of choices to the candidate. The candidate's task is to
select one or more of the choices, up to a maximum of max-choices. The
interaction is always initialized with no choices selected.

The ChoiceInteraction.Type must be bound to a response variable with a
base-type of identifier and single or multiple cardinality.

In a choice interaction, it is considered best practice to use the
shared interaction vocabulary related to the layout of choices instead
of using the optional orientation attribute. If both the shared
interaction vocabulary classes and the orientation attribute are used,
the shared interaction vocabulary classes take precedence over the
orientation attribute value (including the default value of "vertical").

**ChoiceInteraction Attributes (element: qti-choice-interaction)**

| **Name**      | **Usage** | **Value(s)**                      | **Default**   |
|---------------|-----------|-----------------------------------|---------------|
| max-choices   | optional  | Non-negative integer              | 1             |
| min-choices   | optional  | Non-negative integer              | 0 (unlimited) |
| orientation\* | optional  | Vocabulary:* horizontal* vertical | vertical      |
| shuffle       | optional  | boolean                           | false         |

\* Deprecated in favor of the shared interaction vocabulary.

The choice interaction is made up of choices, which use the
qti-simple-choice element (SimpleChoice.Type) to present an ordered list
of choices to the candidate.

As noted in the table above, the "shuffle" attribute is optional and
defaults to false.  However, if the shuffle attribute is set to "true",
and the delivery engine supports the discretionary "shuffle" feature,
the presentation system must randomize the order in which the choices
are originally listed in the Assessment Item XML, subject to the value
of the fixed attribute of each choice. If fixed is "true" for a choice
then the position of this choice within the interaction must not be
changed by the presentation system even if the immediately enclosing
interaction supports the shuffling of choices. If no value is specified
then the choice is free to be shuffled.

**SimpleChoice (element: qti-simple-choice) Attributes**

| **Name**            | **Usage** | **Value(s)**            | **Default** |
|---------------------|-----------|-------------------------|-------------|
| identifier          | required  | Identifier              |             |
| template-identifier | optional  | Identifier              |             |
| show-hide           | optional  | Vocabulary:* show* hide | show        |
| fixed               | optional  | boolean                 | false       |

Here is an example of a choice interaction that allows the candidate to
pick one choice (sometimes called single selection choice).

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>xmlns=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qti/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot; </span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_itemv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> identifier=&quot;beginnersguide007&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> title=&quot;BG true false example &quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> time-dependent=&quot;false&quot; </span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> xml:lang=&quot;en-US&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span><span class="er"> </span>base-type=&quot;identifier&quot; cardinality=&quot;single&quot; identifier=&quot;RESPONSE&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;true&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span><span class="er"> </span>base-type=&quot;float&quot; cardinality=&quot;single&quot; identifier=&quot;SCORE&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;1&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;This is a True/False question?&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span><span class="er"> </span>max-choices=&quot;1&quot; response-identifier=&quot;RESPONSE&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span><span class="er"> </span>identifier=&quot;true&quot;&gt;True&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span><span class="er"> </span>identifier=&quot;false&quot;&gt;False&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span></span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">template=</span><span class="st">&quot;https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/rptemplates/match_correct&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><figure
id="fig-example-of-a-choice-interaction-with-a-single-choice-permitted">
<img
src="/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl3choiceSingle.png"
alt="a choice interaction with two possible choices shown" />
<figcaption>Figure 6 <span class="fig-title">Example of a Choice
Interaction with a single choice permitted.</span></figcaption>
</figure></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Below is an example of a choice interaction that allows the candidate to
select multiple response options to respond to the prompt.

**Example: qti-choice-interaction (multiple cardinality)**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 50%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">  https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;QTI3multiplechoice2&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;QTI 3 Choice with Multiple Cardinality&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en-US&quot;</span> <span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;multiple&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;H&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;O&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">min-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">max-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;6&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;Which of the following elements are used to form water?&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;H&quot;</span>&gt;Hydrogen&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;He&quot;</span>&gt;Helium&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;C&quot;</span>&gt;Carbon&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;O&quot;</span>&gt;Oxygen&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;N&quot;</span>&gt;Nitrogen&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;Cl&quot;</span>&gt;Chlorine&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="ot">template=</span><span class="st">&quot;https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/rptemplates/match_correct&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><figure
id="fig-expected-rendering-choices-rendered-in-a-single-column-vertically-oriented">
<img src="/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl3image75.png"
width="600"
alt="Question with 6 response options presented vertically (each option on a new line)." />
<figcaption>Figure 7 <span class="fig-title"> Expected Rendering:
Choices rendered in a single column, vertically oriented.
</span></figcaption>
</figure></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

By default, in the absence of any shared interaction vocabulary, the
presentation of each choice control, label, label suffix, and
description, is left to the implementer, with the only requirement that
the choices are displayed in a single column, vertically oriented.

The qti-choice-interaction has a considerable amount of its shared
interaction vocabulary dedicated to the presentation/layout of the
interaction's associated qti-simple-choices. Each qti-simple-choice
consists of a collection of elements, displayed from left to right,
defined as follows:

1.  A "control"; e.g., either a radio button or checkbox control, and
2.  A "label"; e.g., often an alphanumeric character such as, "A", "B",
    "1", "2", etc.
3.  A "label suffix"; e.g., a "." or ")" character after the label, and
4.  A "description"; e.g., the HTML content contained by a
    qti-simple-choice element

Go to the [1EdTech QTI 3 Standardized Shared and CSS Classes section on
Choice Interaction
classes](https://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/vocab#choice-interaction)
for full details.

</div>

<div id="text-entry-interaction" class="section">

#### 3.2.3 Text Entry Interaction<a href="#h.5bw8rpbotrcs" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.5bw8rpbotrcs" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

A Text Entry Interaction is an inline interaction that obtains a simple
piece of text from the candidate. Like qti-inline-choice-interaction,
the delivery engine must allow the candidate to review their choice
within the context of the surrounding text.

The TextEntryInteraction.Type (qti-text-entry-interaction) must be bound
to a response variable with single or record cardinality only. If the
response variable has single cardinality the base-type must be one of
string, integer or float; if it has record cardinality the permitted
fields are 'stringValue', 'floatValue', etc.

**TextEntryInteraction Attributes (element:
qti-text-entry-interaction)**

| **Name** | **Usage** | **Value(s)** | **Default** |
|----|----|----|----|
| response-identifier | required | Identifier |  |
| base | optional | xs:int | Default is 10. |
| string-identifier | optional | Identifier of a qti-response-declaration with base-type=string |  |
| expected-length | optional | Non-negative integer |  |
| pattern-mask | optional | string |  |
| placeholder-text | optional | string |  |
| format | optional | Normalized string |  |

Below is an example of a Text Entry Interaction:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>xmlns=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qti/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot; </span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_itemv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> identifier=&quot;bpig010&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> title=&quot;Text Entry example&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> time-dependent=&quot;false&quot; </span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> xml:lang=&quot;en-US&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;York&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-mapping</span> <span class="ot">default-value=</span><span class="st">&quot;0&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">qti-map-entry</span> <span class="ot">map-key=</span><span class="st">&quot;York&quot;</span> <span class="ot">mapped-value=</span><span class="st">&quot;1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">case-sensitive=</span><span class="st">&quot;true&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">qti-map-entry</span> <span class="ot">map-key=</span><span class="st">&quot;york&quot;</span> <span class="ot">mapped-value=</span><span class="st">&quot;0.5&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-mapping</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span><span class="er"> </span>base-type=&quot;float&quot; cardinality=&quot;single&quot; identifier=&quot;SCORE&quot; /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Identify the missing word in this famous quote from Shakespeare&#39;s Richard III.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">blockquote</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Now is the winter of our discontent&lt;<span class="kw">br</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      Made glorious summer by this sun of &lt;<span class="kw">qti-text-entry-interaction</span><span class="er"> </span>response-identifier=&quot;RESPONSE&quot; /&gt;;&lt;<span class="kw">br</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      And all the clouds that lour&#39;d upon our house&lt;<span class="kw">br</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      In the deep bosom of the ocean buried.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;/<span class="kw">blockquote</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span> <span class="ot">template=</span><span class="st">&quot;https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/rptemplates/map_response&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Which might look something like this when presented to candidates:

<figure
id="fig-a-sample-text-entry-item-as-it-might-be-presented-during-an-assessment">
<img src="/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl3textEntry.png"
width="606"
alt="Four sentences are presented with the second sentence having a text entry box at the end of the line." />
<figcaption>Figure 8 <span class="fig-title">A sample text entry item as
it might be presented during an assessment. </span></figcaption>
</figure>

The scoring for this item could have just matched the correct response
(in the response declaration) but actually uses a mapping to enable
partial credit for an improperly capitalized city name (spelled without
a capital letter, or all caps, or other capitilization error). When
mapping strings the mapping, by default, is NOT case sensitive. This
example also illustrates the use of the mapping when the response only
has single cardinality.

The bulk of the shared interaction vocabulary for the Text Entry
interaction adds further definition to the presentation width of the
input element by utilizing the class attribute. Go to the [1EdTech QTI 3
Standardized Shared and CSS Classes section on Text Entry Interaction
classes](https://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/vocab#text-entry-interaction)
for full details.

</div>

<div id="extended-text-interaction" class="section">
