<span id="cb1-96"><a href="#cb1-96" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-if</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-97"><a href="#cb1-97" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-condition</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-98"><a href="#cb1-98" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-condition</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-99"><a href="#cb1-99" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-if</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-100"><a href="#cb1-100" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-not</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-101"><a href="#cb1-101" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-is-null</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-102"><a href="#cb1-102" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">qti-variable</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;response_3&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-103"><a href="#cb1-103" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-is-null</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-104"><a href="#cb1-104" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-not</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-105"><a href="#cb1-105" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-106"><a href="#cb1-106" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-sum</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-107"><a href="#cb1-107" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">qti-variable</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-108"><a href="#cb1-108" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">qti-map-response</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;response_3&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-109"><a href="#cb1-109" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-sum</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-110"><a href="#cb1-110" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-111"><a href="#cb1-111" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE_3&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-112"><a href="#cb1-112" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-map-response</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;response_3&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-113"><a href="#cb1-113" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-114"><a href="#cb1-114" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-if</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-115"><a href="#cb1-115" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-condition</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-116"><a href="#cb1-116" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-117"><a href="#cb1-117" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
<td></td>
</tr>

alt="Two drop down menus are shown, one for the date of birth of Shakespeare, one for the date of death. Below these options is a paragraph of text with an inline text entry box within the text." />
<figcaption>Figure 29 <span class="fig-title">Composite Item
Example</span></figcaption>

</div>

<div id="response-processing-0" class="section">

### 3.4 Response Processing<a href="#h.hsyeo88t451s" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.hsyeo88t451s" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Item response processing occurs after the candidate has completed an
attempt on an item.   The purpose of response processing is to compute
the value of the item's outcome variables. The most common application
is to compute the candidate's score on the item, but in more advanced
items, response processing may be used to show or hide feedback or
interaction choices, and for other purposes.

<div id="response-processing-rules" class="section">

#### 3.4.1 Response Processing Rules<a href="#h.dbczvb5ky3pl" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.dbczvb5ky3pl" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Response processing consists of a sequence of rules that are carried
out, in order, by the response processor. A
[ResponseCondition](http://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/info/index.html#Abstract_ResponseRuleGroup)
 rule is a special type of rule which contains sub-sequences of rules
divided into
[responseIf](http://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/info/index.html#DataAttribute_ResponseCondition_qti-response-if)
,
[responseElseIf](http://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/info/index.html#DataAttribute_ResponseCondition_qti-response-else-if)
 and
[responseElse](http://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/info/index.html#DataAttribute_ResponseCondition_qti-response-else)
 sections. The response processor evaluates the expressions in the
responseIf and responseElseIf elements to determine which sub-sequence
to follow. In this example, the responseIf section is followed only if
the variable with identifier RESPONSE matches the correct response
declared for it. The responseElseIf section is followed if RESPONSE
matches the response explicitly given (which places the correct driver
1st but confuses the other two). Finally, the responseElse section is
followed if neither of the previous two apply. The responseElse section
has no corresponding expression of course. The
[qti-set-outcome-value](http://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/info/index.html#AbstractAttribute_OutcomeRule_qti-set-outcome-value)
 element is just a
[ResponseRule](http://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/info/index.html#RootAttribute_ResponseProcessing_responseRuleGroup)
 that tells the processor to set the value of the specified outcome
variable to the value of the expression it contains.

The
[qti-variable](http://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/info/index.html#AbstractAttribute_ExpressionGroup_qti-variable)
,
[qti-correct](http://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/info/index.html#AbstractAttribute_ExpressionGroup_qti-correct)
 and
[qti-base-value](http://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/info/index.html#AbstractAttribute_ExpressionGroup_qti-base-value)
 elements are examples of simple expressions. In other words,
expressions that are indivisible. In contrast, the
[qti-match](http://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/info/index.html#AbstractAttribute_ExpressionGroup_qti-match)
 and
[qti-ordered](http://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/info/index.html#AbstractAttribute_ExpressionGroup_qti-ordered)
 elements are examples of operators. Operators are expressions that
combine other expressions to form new values. For example, match is used
to form a boolean depending on whether or not two expressions have
matching values.

</div>

<div id="fixed-and-general-response-processing" class="section">

#### 3.4.2 Fixed and General Response Processing<a href="#h.66wn7ucnbn4v" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.66wn7ucnbn4v" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

There are two forms of response processing:

1.  Fixed Template Response Processing
2.  General Response Processing.

In  "fixed template" response processing, the processing to be done is
identified by a "template name".   There are three standard template
names, and a delivery engine may define additional template names.   For
simple scenarios, the "match_correct" and "map_response" standard
templates may be used because this maximizes interoperability.   All
conformant delivery systems are required to provide response processing
as defined by these two templates, while there is no assurance that a
template other than these two will be supported, or that general
response processing will be supported.

The three standard "fixed" response processing templates are:

|  |  |  |
|----|----|----|
| Template Name | Description | Location |
| match_correct | https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/rptemplates/match_correct.xml |  |
| map_response | https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/rptemplates/map_response.xml |  |
| map_response_point | https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/rptemplates/map_response_point.xml |  |

For "general" response processing, QTI defines an XML-based Domain
Specific Language (DSL) with a large number of commands and operators
for computing and setting template and outcome variables. General
response processing is necessary for more complex items, such as items
generated from templates, adaptive items involving feedback, and
composite items.

The same Domain Specific Language is also used for Outcome Processing,
Template Defaults, Constraints, and Processing, and for evaluating
Preconditions and Branch Rules.

</div>

<div id="partial-scoring" class="section">

#### 3.4.3 Partial Scoring<a href="#h.na7tc1frjtwv" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.na7tc1frjtwv" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Please see section [3.3 Composite Items](#h.fzolwwxsh7ga) for an example
of response processing that implements partial scoring.

</div>

<div id="conformance-0" class="section">

#### 3.4.4 Conformance<a href="#h.ceoxmo4w0ssj" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.ceoxmo4w0ssj" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

It is possible to define the effect of the standard templates using the
"general" response processing DSL, but it is not required that a QTI 3
Entry-level delivery system implement the standard templates through
support of the DSL. A "built-in" implementation of the standard
templates is sufficient for conformance in a QTI 3 Entry-level delivery
system.

At the QTI 3 Core-level of conformance, a delivery system is required to
support enough of the response processing DSL to be able to load and
execute at least the "match_correct" and "map_response" standard
templates, as well as other templates or inline response processing
blocks using the same subset of the Domain Specific Language as these
two standard templates.  For convenience and performance, a QTI 3
Core-level delivery system may still also use the "built-in" approach
for some of the templates which it supports, in addition to supporting
the response processing language.

</div>

<div id="external-scoring" class="section">

#### 3.4.5 External Scoring<a href="#h.i6qwpp7ykfer" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.i6qwpp7ykfer" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

In some cases, response processing is undertaken by external systems or
human scorers. This is typically the case for items asking candidates to
write an essay. However, it might be important for external systems or
human scorers to know which outcome value has to be set to derive an
appropriate score.

**External Scoring Example**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="er"> </span>  xmlns=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot; identifier=&quot;essay&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     title=&quot;Write an essay&quot; adaptive=&quot;false&quot; time-dependent=&quot;false&quot; xml:lang=&quot;en-US&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span> <span class="er"> </span>external-scored=&quot;human&quot;/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;stem&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        In the classroom, we discovered Martin Luther King Jr. and his devotion.</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-extended-text-interaction</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;Write an abstract about the life of this historical figure.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-extended-text-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

This example describes an item with a single
qti-extended-text-interactionasking the candidate to write an essay. As
the item does not contain qti-response-processing, the SCORE
qti-outcome-declaration has its external-scored attribute value set to
"human". This makes QTI compliant systems aware that the final value of
SCORE has to be set by a human scorer after the Item Session has closed.

</div>

</div>

<div id="outcomes" class="section">

### 3.5 Outcomes<a href="#h.886q73e2ya9q" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.886q73e2ya9q" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Outcome variables are declared by outcome declarations. Their value is
set either from a default given in the declaration itself or by a
responseRule during responseProcessing.

Items that declare a numeric outcome variable representing the
candidate's overall performance on the item should use the "SCORE"
built-in outcome variable. SCORE must have a base-type of float.

Items that declare a maximum score (in multiple response choice
interactions, for example) should do so by declaring the built-in
variable named "MAXSCORE".  MAXSCORE must have a base-type of float.

Items or tests that want to make the fact that the candidate scored
above a predefined threshold available as a variable should use the
built-in "PASSED' outcome variable. PASSED must have a base-type of
boolean.

At runtime, outcome variables are instantiated as part of an item
session. Their values may be initialized with a default value and/or set
during response processing. If no default value is given in the
declaration then the outcome variable is initialized to NULL
*unless* the outcome is of a numeric type (integer or float) in which
case it is initialized to 0.

For non-adaptive Items; i.e., items that do not use template processing
to compute default values, the values of the outcome variables are reset
to their default values prior to each invocation of response processing.
For adaptive Items, the outcome variables *retain* the values that were
assigned to them during the previous invocation of response processing.
For more information, see section [3.4.2 Fixed and General Response
Processing](#h.66wn7ucnbn4v) .

There is one built-in outcome variable, "completionStatus", that is
declared implicitly and must not appear in an outcomeDeclaration.
Delivery Engines must maintain the value of the built-in outcome
variable completionStatus, a single identifier. It starts with the
reserved value "not_attempted". At the start of the first attempt it
changes to the reserved value "unknown". It remains with this value for
the duration of the item session unless set to a different value by a
setOutcomeValue rule in responseProcessing. There are four permitted
values:

- 'completed' - the candidate has experienced enough of the item to
  consider it completed;
- 'incomplete' - the candidate has not experienced enough of the item to
  consider it completed;
- 'not_attempted' - the candidate is considered to have not used the
  item in any significant way;
- 'unknown' - no assertion on the state of completion can be made.

Any one of these values may be set during response processing. If an
Adaptive Item sets completionStatus to completed then the session must
be placed into the closed state, however an item session is not required
to wait for the completed signal before terminating, it may terminate in
response to a direct request from the candidate, through running out of
time or through some other exceptional circumstance. Adaptive Items must
maintain a suitable value and should set completionStatus to "completed"
to indicate when the cycle of interaction, response processing and
feedback must stop. Non-adaptive Items are not required to set a value
for completionStatus, but they may do so. Delivery Engines are
encouraged to use the value of completionStatus when communicating
externally.

**OutcomeDeclaration Attributes (element: qti-outcome-declaration)**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th><strong>Name</strong></th>
<th><strong>Usage</strong></th>
<th><strong>Value(s)</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>identifier</td>
<td>required</td>
<td>Identifier</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>base-type</td>
<td>required</td>
<td><p>Normalized string</p>
<ul>
<li>identifier</li>
<li>boolean</li>
<li>integer</li>
<li>float</li>
<li>string</li>
<li>point</li>
<li>pair</li>
<li>directedPair</li>
<li>duration</li>
<li>file</li>
<li>uri</li>
</ul></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>cardinality</td>
<td>required</td>
<td><p>Normalized string</p>
<ul>
<li>single</li>
<li>multiple</li>
<li>ordered</li>
<li>record</li>
</ul></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>view</td>
<td>optional</td>
<td><p>Normalized string</p>
<ul>
<li>author</li>
<li>candidate</li>
<li>proctor</li>
<li>scorer</li>
<li>testConstructor</li>
<li>tutor</li>
</ul></td>
<td>The intended audience for an outcome variable can be set with the
view attribute. If no view is specified the outcome is treated as
relevant to <em>all</em> views. Complex items, such as adaptive items or
complex templates, may declare outcomes that are of no interest to the
candidate at all, but are merely used to hold intermediate values or
other information useful during the item or test session. Such variables
should be declared with a view of author (for item outcomes) or
testConstructor (for test outcomes). Systems may exclude outcomes from
result reports on the basis of their declared view if appropriate. Where
more than one class of user should be able to view an outcome variable
the view attribute should contain a comma delimited list.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>interpretation</td>
<td>optional</td>
<td>string</td>
<td>A human interpretation of the variable's value.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>long-interpretation</td>
<td>optional</td>
<td>uri</td>
<td><p>An optional link to an extended interpretation of the outcome
variable's value.         </p>
<p>Declared outcomes with numeric types should indicate their range of
possible values using normal-maximum and normal-minimum, especially if
this range differs from [0,1].</p>
<p>Declared outcomes with numeric types should indicate their range of
possible values using normal-maximum and normal-minimum, especially if
this range differs from [0,1].</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>normal-maximum</td>
<td>optional</td>
<td>float</td>
<td>The normal-maximum attribute optionally defines the maximum
<em>magnitude</em> of numeric outcome variables, it must be a positive
value. If given, the outcome's value can be divided by normal-maximum
and then truncated (if necessary) to obtain a normalized score in the
range [-1.0,1.0]. normal-maximum has no effect on response processing or
the values that the outcome variable itself can take.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>normal-minimum</td>
<td>optional</td>
<td>float</td>
<td>The normal-minimum attribute optionally defines the minimum value of
numeric outcome variables, it may be negative.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>mastery-value</td>
<td>optional</td>
<td>float</td>
<td>The mastery-value attribute optionally defines a value for numeric
outcome variables above which the aspect being measured is considered to
have been mastered by the candidate.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="stylesheets-0" class="section">

### 3.6 Stylesheets<a href="#h.fu8olo1248hn" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.fu8olo1248hn" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

QTI 3 introduces an extensive shared interaction vocabulary for
improving item interaction rendering interoperability. To further
improve item rendering interoperability, QTI 3 also introduces a limited
number of shared CSS style names and conventions for use by authoring
and delivery systems. In the [QTI 3 Standardized Shared Vocabulary and
CSS Classes](https://www.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/vocab) document,
the shared vocabulary are defined (with examples) and sample CSS code is
provided. In addition to interaction specific vocabulary, the document
includes:

- Accessibility Classes
- The standardized markup for inline text
- Content layout (rows, columns, offsets, etc.)
- Alignment markup
- Vertical Writing
- Markup for visually hiding elements
- List Styles

By implementing these shared styles and conventions, QTI 3 authoring and
delivery platforms can safely import/export - or render - items while
preserving certain presentation characteristics without using custom
stylesheet injection. Note that all of the shared styles are prefaced by
the "qti-" string so as to avoid collisions with a delivery platform's
existing CSS.

Reference implementations of each CSS class - and further examples - can
be found in the IMS GitHub QTI 3 repository. Over time, it is expected
that additional shared CSS style names and definitions will be added to
this repository.

<div id="custom-styles" class="section">

#### 3.6.1 Custom Styles

For QTI 3, the best practice is to avoid using custom styles, and
instead use the Shared CSS Vocabulary as described above. By using the
Shared CSS style names, Test Delivery Systems can optimize the rendering
experience for accessibility and for design responsiveness.

However, *as a last resort*, custom stylesheets may be injected into an
item and other QTI content, such as tests, stimuli, and feedback,
rubric, and template blocks, using the qti-stylesheet element:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-stylesheet</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;samples/Quizzco-Custom-Styles.css&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;text/css&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">div</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;Quizzco__item123&quot;</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-layout-row&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      ...</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

As in earlier versions of QTI, it is not required for conformance for
delivery systems to support the *qti-stylesheet* element -- a further
reason why stylesheet injection is likely not to be interoperable.
 Notwithstanding these issues, if your implementation will be using
stylesheets,  QTI 3 recommends the use of CSS 3 stylesheets. For more
information on CSS 3 stylesheets, see the W3C documentation at
<https://www.w3schools.com/css/default.asp>  .

The best practice convention in QTI 3 is that stylesheets referenced
within an AssssmentItem file apply to the item itself, and are not
assumed to apply to associated content such as stimulus (
*qti-assessment-stimulus*) or rubric blocks ( *qti-rubric-block)*, both
of which can have their own associated stylesheets. An item's
stylesheets are assumed to apply to content within *qti-item-body,
qti-catalog-info,*  as well as to any content generated from the use of
feedback or templates. Any included content using XInclude that is
placed within the *qti-item-body, qti-catalog-info,* and
*qti-modal-feedback*, or *qti-template-block* generated content is also
assumed to be covered by the stylesheet reference made within an item.

It is the responsibility of an item author to limit the scope of an
injected stylesheet to the content with which it is associated. Because
of the way CSS scoping is defined, this is admittedly difficult.  The
following describes some approaches to limiting CSS scope:

- **Use class naming conventions.** Use a naming convention for CSS
  classes which includes a prefix with an item vendor-specific code, and
  which is likely to make custom  class names globally unique.
- **Avoid bare element or attribute selectors.** Avoid CSS style rules
  which apply broadly, such as those using "bare" element or attribute
  selectors.  Such rules are very likely to have a wider impact than
  intended.  Do not write rules like "div {color: red}". There are bound
  to be many other *div's*in the document, not all of which should be
  red.  Instead, write ".vendorprefix-red { color: red }", and add the
  "vendorprefix-red" class to elements in the content which should be
  red.   Further note: at all costs, avoid specifying any colors in
  custom style definitions as these are practically guaranteed to result
  in an accessibility problem for the delivery platform.
- **Do not assume defaults are in effect.** Do not assume that CSS
  properties will have their default values.  If it is important for an
  element to have the default CSS property values, you should set the
  properties explicitly to the default values.
- **Use id attributes and descendent selectors.** QTI 3 allows the use
  of the *id* attribute on HTML5 elements.  It may be helpful to assign
  an id to a higher level element within the HTML content you are
  styling, such as a *div,*and write style rules against that id,
  possibly combined with descendant selectors. Choose ids that are
  likely to be globally unique so that other stylesheets in the
  environment don't affect your content, and vice-versa.
- **Do not assume order.**Do not assume an injected stylesheet will be
  first or last, or in any particular position relative to other
  stylesheets in the delivery environment. That is, if item 1 injects
  stylesheet A, and the next item, item 2, injects stylesheet B, a
  delivery engine is free to insert the two stylesheets in any order,
  and an author cannot rely on stylesheet B being after stylesheet A.
  Even worse, stylesheet C, from somewhere else entirely, may land in
  between the two. In short, it is not really possible to rely on the
  CSS cascade between stylesheets, because the ultimate ordering of the
  stylesheets is not defined.
- **Be aware of delivery system artifacts**. You may observe that the
  HTML generated by the delivery system for QTI content includes HTML
  elements which were not in the original content.  This is because
  delivery engines commonly transform QTI content such as interactions,
  interaction components (e.g. *qti-prompt*, or *qti-simple-choice*),
  the *qti-item-body*, catalog cards, rubrics, feedback blocks, etc,
  into standard HTML elements, and these will end up as the parents,
  children, or siblings of the HTML elements placed in the item
  directly.  It is unwise to style these  system-generated elements or
  to rely on the generated structure in CSS style rules, because another
  delivery system, or even another version of the same delivery system,
  will generate a different structure.  Be aware when writing style
  rules that delivery system artifacts may appear "out of nowhere" and
  "get in the way" of sibling selectors, child selectors, adjacency
  selectors, first- and last-child pseudo-selectors, and the like.
- **Do not depend on the stylesheet.** To be interoperable, QTI content
  should fall back to a functional and accessible presentation even if
  the stylesheet is not injected. When injecting a stylesheet, authors
  should still rely on the shared interaction vocabulary and shared CSS
  classes for most of the styling and reserve the additional rules in
  the stylesheet for styling which cannot be achieved through shared
  interaction vocabulary and shared CSS classes. An author should ensure
  that the content is acceptable using only shared interaction
  vocabulary and shared CSS classes, and be prepared for a stylesheet
  not to be injected at all.

Here is an example item, generated by a fictional vendor Quizzco, with
some of these best practices applied:

Item with External Stylesheet Example

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qti/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;QTI3-custom-stylesheet&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;Custom Stylesheet&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en-US&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;Y&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;Y&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-stylesheet</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;samples/Quizzco-Custom-Styles.css&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;text/css&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">div</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;Quizzco__item123&quot;</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-layout-row&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">div</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-layout-col7&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;Quizzco__item__prompt&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      Identify the missing word in this famous quote from Shakespeare&#39;s Richard III.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       &lt;<span class="kw">blockquote</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;Quizzco__item__passage&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        Now is the winter of our discontent&lt;<span class="kw">br</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        Made glorious summer by this sun of </span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-inline-choice-interaction</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-inline-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;G&quot;</span>&gt;Gloucester&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-inline-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-inline-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;L&quot;</span>&gt;Lancaster&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-inline-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-inline-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;Y&quot;</span>&gt;York&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-inline-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-inline-choice-interaction</span>&gt;;&lt;<span class="kw">br</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        And all the clouds that lour&#39;d upon our house&lt;<span class="kw">br</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-34"><a href="#cb1-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        In the deep bosom of the ocean buried.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-35"><a href="#cb1-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">blockquote</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-36"><a href="#cb1-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-37"><a href="#cb1-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-38"><a href="#cb1-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-39"><a href="#cb1-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-40"><a href="#cb1-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   <span class="ot">template=</span><span class="st">&quot;https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/rptemplates/match_correct.xml&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-41"><a href="#cb1-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

**External Stylesheet Example:** Quizzco-Custom-Styles.css

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs css"><code class="sourceCode css"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="pp">#Quizzco__item123</span> Quizzco__item__prompt {</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     all<span class="ch">:</span> <span class="bu">initial</span><span class="op">;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     display<span class="ch">:</span> <span class="dv">block</span><span class="op">;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     margin-block-start<span class="ch">:</span> <span class="dv">1</span><span class="dt">em</span><span class="op">;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     text-align<span class="ch">:</span> <span class="dv">start</span><span class="op">;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     padding<span class="ch">:</span> <span class="dv">5</span><span class="dt">px</span><span class="op">;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     font-weight<span class="ch">:</span> <span class="dv">normal</span><span class="op">;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  }</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="pp">#Quizzco__item123</span> Quizzco__item__passage {</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     line-height<span class="ch">:</span> <span class="dv">1.3</span><span class="dt">em</span><span class="op">;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     font-weight<span class="ch">:</span> <span class="dv">bolder</span><span class="op">;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  }</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

</div>

<div id="advanced-item-structures" class="section">

### 3.7 Advanced Item Structures<a href="#h.9t0ualvxoz7o" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.9t0ualvxoz7o" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

<div id="customized-response-processing" class="section">

#### 3.7.1 Customized Response Processing<a href="#h.fi29q8dubjgw" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.fi29q8dubjgw" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The qti-custom-operator extension mechanism allows the inclusion of
custom, non-QTI response evaluation rules.  In general, such APIs are
likely to be particular to a specific software library or programming
language. For that reason, it is difficult to predict what form such
custom operators will take, and, by extension, how to generalise
functions or syntax between different custom operators.

By way of illustration, the following fragment illustrates the use of
the Maxima engine as a response processing library via the
qti-custom-operator element.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;oDummy&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-custom-operator</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;org.qtitools.mathassess.ScriptRule&quot;</span> <span class="ot">ma:simplify=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="er"> </span> ma:syntax=&quot;text/x-maxima&quot; xmlns:ma=&quot;http://mathassess.qtitools.org/xsd/mathassess&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-base-value</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span>&gt;<span class="er">&lt;</span>[!CDATA[</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              oInput:RESPONSE;        </span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              equalp(p,q):= block([simp:false], if p=q then return(true)</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                   else return(false) )$</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               isEqual: equalp(RESPONSE,mAns);</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              equivp(p,q):= block([simp:true], if is(equal(p,q))=true then return(true)</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      else return(false) )$</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              isEquiv: equivp(RESPONSE,mAns);        </span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              isRecip: equivp(RESPONSE,1/mAns);</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              numOrig: equivp(num(RESPONSE),mNum);</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              denomOrig: equivp(denom(RESPONSE),mDen);                </span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              isOrig: if (numOrig and denomOrig) then true else false;        </span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              denR1: equivp(denom(RESPONSE),1);</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              denR2: equalp(denom(RESPONSE),iN^(-iC));</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              numR1: equalp(num(RESPONSE),iN^iC);</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              numR2: equivp(num(RESPONSE),1);                </span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              negPower: is(ev(-iC,numer,simp)&gt;0);</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              isSimp: equalp(RESPONSE,ev(RESPONSE,simp));</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              isNotSimp: if((numR2 and denR2 and not negPower) or (isEquiv and not isSimp)) then true</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                     else false;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              isOK: if ((numR1 and denR1) or (negPower and numR2 and denR2)) then true</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                        else false;        </span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              isAdded: equivp(RESPONSE,mAdd);</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              isSubtracted: equivp(RESPONSE,mSub);</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              isMultiplied: equivp(RESPONSE,mMult);</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="er">]]&gt;</span>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-base-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-custom-operator</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

In this case, a qti-custom-operator is used as a very slim container for
what is effectively a complete script in Maxima's language. A QTI
processor designed to work with this qti-custom-operator could pass the
script verbatim to Maxima, and use its response to set the 'oDummy'
outcome value.

</div>

<div id="adaptive-items" class="section">

#### 3.7.2 Adaptive Items<a href="#h.pd5mfepf6mk9" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.pd5mfepf6mk9" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Adaptive items are a feature that allows an item to be scored adaptively
over a sequence of attempts. This allows the candidate to alter their
answer following feedback or to be posed additional questions based on
their current answer. Response processing works differently for adaptive
items. Normally (for non-adaptive items) each attempt is independent and
the outcome variables are set to their default values each time response
processing is carried out. For adaptive items, the outcome variables
retain their values across multiple attempts and are only updated by
subsequent response processing. This difference is indicated by the
value of the adaptive attribute of the Assessment Item. Adaptive items
must of course provide feedback to the candidate in order to allow them
to adjust their response(s).

In the following example, the feedback is used to contain a solution
which is displayed when the candidate clicks the "Show Solution" button.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">xmlns:m=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0 </span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">  https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0_v1p0.xsd </span></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">  http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML </span></span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">  https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/mathml/v3p0/schema/xsd/mathml3.xsd&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;Example03-feedbackBlock-solution&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;Using feedbackBlock to show a solution&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">adaptive=</span><span class="st">&quot;true&quot;</span> <span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en-US&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">tool-name=</span><span class="st">&quot;Mathqurate&quot;</span> <span class="ot">tool-version=</span><span class="st">&quot;0.9.7b&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;7.389&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co">&lt;!-- This response variable is set to true if the solution button is clicked.--&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;boolean&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SOLREQUEST&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;multiple&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;FEEDBACK&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;EMPTY&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">normal-maximum=</span><span class="st">&quot;10.0&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   <span class="ot">normal-minimum=</span><span class="st">&quot;0.0&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;0.0&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co">&lt;!-- This outcome variable is set to true if the solution has been viewed.--&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;boolean&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;seenSolution&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;false&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-34"><a href="#cb1-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-35"><a href="#cb1-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb1-36"><a href="#cb1-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co">&lt;!-- This outcome variable controls the display of the solution button.--&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-37"><a href="#cb1-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ASKSOLUTION&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-38"><a href="#cb1-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-39"><a href="#cb1-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;asksolution&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-40"><a href="#cb1-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-41"><a href="#cb1-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-42"><a href="#cb1-42" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-43"><a href="#cb1-43" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-44"><a href="#cb1-44" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Find the value of &lt;<span class="kw">math</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML&quot;</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;mathML0&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-45"><a href="#cb1-45" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">semantics</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-46"><a href="#cb1-46" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">mrow</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">msup</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">mi</span>&gt;e&lt;/<span class="kw">mi</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">mn</span>&gt;2&lt;/<span class="kw">mn</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">msup</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">mrow</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-47"><a href="#cb1-47" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">annotation</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;LaTeX&quot;</span>&gt;[e^2]&lt;/<span class="kw">annotation</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-48"><a href="#cb1-48" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">semantics</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">math</span>&gt; to 3 decimal places.</span>
<span id="cb1-49"><a href="#cb1-49" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-50"><a href="#cb1-50" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">div</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-margin-b-2&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-51"><a href="#cb1-51" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">table</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-52"><a href="#cb1-52" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">tbody</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-53"><a href="#cb1-53" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">tr</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-54"><a href="#cb1-54" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">td</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-padding-e-2&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-55"><a href="#cb1-55" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                &lt;<span class="kw">qti-text-entry-interaction</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-input-width-10&quot;</span> <span class="ot">pattern-mask=</span><span class="st">&quot;([0-9.\-]{0,10})&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-56"><a href="#cb1-56" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                  <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;textEntryInteraction0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">label=</span><span class="st">&quot;mathInput&quot;</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-57"><a href="#cb1-57" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;/<span class="kw">td</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-58"><a href="#cb1-58" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">td</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-59"><a href="#cb1-59" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                &lt;<span class="kw">qti-feedback-inline</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;feedbackInline0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;CORRECT&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-60"><a href="#cb1-60" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                  <span class="ot">outcome-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;FEEDBACK&quot;</span> <span class="ot">show-hide=</span><span class="st">&quot;show&quot;</span>&gt;Correct &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-feedback-inline</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-61"><a href="#cb1-61" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                &lt;<span class="kw">qti-feedback-inline</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;feedbackInline1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;INCORRECT&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-62"><a href="#cb1-62" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                  <span class="ot">outcome-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;FEEDBACK&quot;</span> <span class="ot">show-hide=</span><span class="st">&quot;show&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-63"><a href="#cb1-63" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                   No, that is not the correct answer</span>
<span id="cb1-64"><a href="#cb1-64" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-feedback-inline</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-65"><a href="#cb1-65" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;/<span class="kw">td</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-66"><a href="#cb1-66" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;/<span class="kw">tr</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-67"><a href="#cb1-67" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">tbody</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-68"><a href="#cb1-68" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">table</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-69"><a href="#cb1-69" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-70"><a href="#cb1-70" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb1-71"><a href="#cb1-71" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      <span class="co">&lt;!-- this feedbackBlock contains the solution --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-72"><a href="#cb1-72" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-feedback-block</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;&quot;</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;feedbackBlock2&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SOLUTION&quot;</span> <span class="ot">outcome-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;FEEDBACK&quot;</span> <span class="ot">show-hide=</span><span class="st">&quot;show&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-73"><a href="#cb1-73" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-content-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-74"><a href="#cb1-74" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">div</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-well&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-75"><a href="#cb1-75" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">math</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML&quot;</span> <span class="ot">display=</span><span class="st">&quot;block&quot;</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;mathML1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-76"><a href="#cb1-76" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                &lt;<span class="kw">semantics</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-77"><a href="#cb1-77" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                  &lt;<span class="kw">mtable</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-78"><a href="#cb1-78" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                        &lt;<span class="kw">mtr</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-79"><a href="#cb1-79" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                          &lt;<span class="kw">mtd</span> <span class="ot">columnalign=</span><span class="st">&quot;right&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-80"><a href="#cb1-80" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                            &lt;<span class="kw">msup</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">mi</span>&gt;e&lt;/<span class="kw">mi</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">mn</span>&gt;2&lt;/<span class="kw">mn</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">msup</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-81"><a href="#cb1-81" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                          &lt;/<span class="kw">mtd</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-82"><a href="#cb1-82" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                          &lt;<span class="kw">mtd</span> <span class="ot">columnalign=</span><span class="st">&quot;center&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-83"><a href="#cb1-83" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                            &lt;<span class="kw">mo</span>&gt;=&lt;/<span class="kw">mo</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-84"><a href="#cb1-84" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                          &lt;/<span class="kw">mtd</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-85"><a href="#cb1-85" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                          &lt;<span class="kw">mtd</span> <span class="ot">columnalign=</span><span class="st">&quot;left&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-86"><a href="#cb1-86" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                            &lt;<span class="kw">mi</span>&gt;exp&lt;/<span class="kw">mi</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">mfenced</span> <span class="ot">close=</span><span class="st">&quot;)&quot;</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">mn</span>&gt;2&lt;/<span class="kw">mn</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">mfenced</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-87"><a href="#cb1-87" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                          &lt;/<span class="kw">mtd</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-88"><a href="#cb1-88" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                        &lt;/<span class="kw">mtr</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-89"><a href="#cb1-89" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                        &lt;<span class="kw">mtr</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-90"><a href="#cb1-90" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                          &lt;<span class="kw">mtd</span> <span class="ot">columnalign=</span><span class="st">&quot;right&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-91"><a href="#cb1-91" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                          &lt;<span class="kw">mtd</span> <span class="ot">columnalign=</span><span class="st">&quot;center&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-92"><a href="#cb1-92" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                            &lt;<span class="kw">mo</span>&gt;=&lt;/<span class="kw">mo</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-93"><a href="#cb1-93" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                          &lt;/<span class="kw">mtd</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-94"><a href="#cb1-94" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                          &lt;<span class="kw">mtd</span> <span class="ot">columnalign=</span><span class="st">&quot;left&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-95"><a href="#cb1-95" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                            &lt;<span class="kw">mi</span>&gt;7.389&lt;/<span class="kw">mi</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-96"><a href="#cb1-96" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                          &lt;/<span class="kw">mtd</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-97"><a href="#cb1-97" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                        &lt;/<span class="kw">mtr</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-98"><a href="#cb1-98" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                    &lt;/<span class="kw">mtable</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-99"><a href="#cb1-99" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                  &lt;<span class="kw">annotation</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;SnuggleTeX&quot;</span>&gt;\begin{eqnarray*}e^2 </span>
<span id="cb1-100"><a href="#cb1-100" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                   <span class="dv">&amp;amp;</span>=<span class="dv">&amp;amp;</span> exp(2) <span class="dv">&amp;amp;</span>=<span class="dv">&amp;amp;</span> 7.389 \end{eqnarray*}</span>
<span id="cb1-101"><a href="#cb1-101" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                  &lt;/<span class="kw">annotation</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-102"><a href="#cb1-102" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                &lt;/<span class="kw">semantics</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-103"><a href="#cb1-103" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;/<span class="kw">math</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-104"><a href="#cb1-104" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-105"><a href="#cb1-105" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-content-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-106"><a href="#cb1-106" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-feedback-block</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-107"><a href="#cb1-107" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb1-108"><a href="#cb1-108" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      <span class="co">&lt;!-- Explain why the score is zero (after viewing solution) --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-109"><a href="#cb1-109" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-feedback-block</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SEEN-SOLUTION&quot;</span> <span class="ot">outcome-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;FEEDBACK&quot;</span> <span class="ot">show-hide=</span><span class="st">&quot;show&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-110"><a href="#cb1-110" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-content-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-111"><a href="#cb1-111" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Since you have viewed the solution, your score for this question will be 0.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-112"><a href="#cb1-112" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-content-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-113"><a href="#cb1-113" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-feedback-block</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-114"><a href="#cb1-114" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb1-115"><a href="#cb1-115" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      <span class="co">&lt;!-- Show the solution button --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-116"><a href="#cb1-116" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-feedback-block</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;asksolution&quot;</span> <span class="ot">outcome-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ASKSOLUTION&quot;</span> <span class="ot">show-hide=</span><span class="st">&quot;show&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-117"><a href="#cb1-117" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-content-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-118"><a href="#cb1-118" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-119"><a href="#cb1-119" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-end-attempt-interaction</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SOLREQUEST&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;Show Solution&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-120"><a href="#cb1-120" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-121"><a href="#cb1-121" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-content-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-122"><a href="#cb1-122" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-feedback-block</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-123"><a href="#cb1-123" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-124"><a href="#cb1-124" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-125"><a href="#cb1-125" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-126"><a href="#cb1-126" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;FEEDBACK&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-127"><a href="#cb1-127" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-multiple</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-128"><a href="#cb1-128" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-variable</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;EMPTY&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-129"><a href="#cb1-129" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-multiple</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-130"><a href="#cb1-130" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-131"><a href="#cb1-131" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-condition</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-132"><a href="#cb1-132" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-if</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-133"><a href="#cb1-133" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-variable</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SOLREQUEST&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-134"><a href="#cb1-134" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="co">&lt;!-- In response processing, the outcome variable FEEDBACK is set to the identifier</span></span>
<span id="cb1-135"><a href="#cb1-135" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">             (SOLUTION) of the solution feedbackBlock, so that the solution appears.--&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-136"><a href="#cb1-136" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;FEEDBACK&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-137"><a href="#cb1-137" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-multiple</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-138"><a href="#cb1-138" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-base-value</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span>&gt;SOLUTION&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-base-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-139"><a href="#cb1-139" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-multiple</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-140"><a href="#cb1-140" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-141"><a href="#cb1-141" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="co">&lt;!-- The seenSolution flag is set to true so that the message about the score will</span></span>
<span id="cb1-142"><a href="#cb1-142" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">             appear if an answer is submitted.--&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-143"><a href="#cb1-143" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;seenSolution&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-144"><a href="#cb1-144" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-base-value</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;boolean&quot;</span>&gt;true&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-base-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-145"><a href="#cb1-145" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-146"><a href="#cb1-146" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="co">&lt;!-- The built-in outcome variable completionStatus is set to completed - this must</span></span>
<span id="cb1-147"><a href="#cb1-147" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">             happen at some stage in any adaptive question otherwise the question is never</span></span>
<span id="cb1-148"><a href="#cb1-148" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">             complete, which is a problem in tests.--&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-149"><a href="#cb1-149" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;completionStatus&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-150"><a href="#cb1-150" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-base-value</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span>&gt;completed&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-base-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-151"><a href="#cb1-151" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span>&gt;</span>
