<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">file</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;passages/stylesheets/unbelievable_night.css&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">resource</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">resource</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;Item1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;exemplar06.xml&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;imsqti_qtiitem_xmlv3p0&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">file</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;exemplar06.xml&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">dependency</span> <span class="ot">identifierref=</span><span class="st">&quot;Stimulus1&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">resource</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">resource</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;Item2&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;imsqti_qtiitem_xmlv3p0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;exemplar04.xml&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">file</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;exemplar04.xml&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">dependency</span> <span class="ot">identifierref=</span><span class="st">&quot;Stimulus1&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">resource</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">resource</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;Item3&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;imsqti_qtiitem_xmlv3p0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;exemplar05.xml&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">file</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;exemplar05.xml&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">dependency</span> <span class="ot">identifierref=</span><span class="st">&quot;Stimulus1&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">resource</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">resources</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">manifest</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="companion-materials-0" class="section">

#### 3.7.8 Companion Materials<a href="#h.cog67s90rmps" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.cog67s90rmps" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Companion materials are described within the
\<qti-companion-materials-info\> node, and include assessment materials
that are required to be available to test takers while answering a
specific item. Materials may include interactive tools, like calculators
or rulers. The specific materials tags, and their best practice usage
are described below.

<div id="calculators" class="section">

##### 3.7.8.1 Calculators<a href="#h.ne0sfxtiw325" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.ne0sfxtiw325" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

All calculators would have access to all number digits, decimal key,
equals button, and Clear button. Spoken (read aloud) capability should
be something that is configurable to either allow, or not allow, during
testing. Additionally, some programs allow for reading the numbers or
functions as you use them, but do not allow reading the number as a
whole. This is usually for Math-related content. The four possible
calculators that can be specified are Basic, Standard, Scientific, and
Graphing. Descriptions of each are included below.

<span style="font-size:1.1em; font-weight:bold;">Basic
Calculator:</span>In the \<qti-calculator-type\> tag, use the "Basic"
vocabulary. The best practice assumed functions: Add, Subtract,
Multiply, Divide.

Example usage:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-companion-materials-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-calculator</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-calculator-type</span>&gt;Basic&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-calculator-type</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-description</span>&gt;4 function calculator&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-description</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-calculator</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-companion-materials-info</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

**Standard Calculator:**In the \<qti-calculator-type\> tag, use the
"Standard" vocabulary. The best practice assumed functions: all basic
calculator functions, Square root (√), Percentage (%) , Plus/Minus
(a.k.a. Sign Change), Memory Functions.

**Scientific Calculator:**In the \<qti-calculator-type\> tag, use the
"Scientific" vocabulary. The functions may include, but are not limited
to: ALL standard calculator functions, a *p* key, square ( *x*2) , cube
(x3),  x to the y (xy), cube root , xth root , logarithm keys, log, ln,
base 10, base *e,*Trigonometry function keys with an INVERSE key for the
inverse functions, sin, cos, tan, hsin (hyperbolic sin), hcos,
(hyperbolic cos), htan (hyperbolic tan), DEG, RAD, GRAD conversion, a
capacity to work in both degree and radian mode, a reciprocal key (1/
*x*) - calculate the inverse of the displayed value, permutation and/or
combination keys ( *n*P *r* , *n*C *r*), parentheses keys, metric
conversion, permutation and combination keys, nPr,  cPr, x!

**Graphing Calculator:**In the \<qti-calculator-type\> tag, use the
"Graphing" vocabulary. A Graphing calculator includes many of the same
functions of a scientific calculator, plus the ability to display
equations graphically.

</div>

<div id="rule" class="section">

##### 3.7.8.2 Rule<a href="#h.j5az5lisqry4" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.j5az5lisqry4" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Allows for the presentation of a measuring device for use on the
computer with the supplied content. Use the qti-description tag for text
description, as a human readable description of the
functionality/capability of the rule. Provide the system of measurement
using the rule system (which provides for choosing between metric (SI)
and US measurement systems), then set the minimum length of the rule,
the minor increment of the rule, and the major increment of the rule
using the unit type (related to the rule measurement system).

Example usage:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-companion-materials-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-rule</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-description</span>&gt;A metric ruler with increments on one side of the rule.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-description</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-rule-system-SI</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;<span class="kw">qti-minimum-length</span>&gt;10&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-minimum-length</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;<span class="kw">qti-minor-increment</span> <span class="ot">unit=</span><span class="st">&quot;Meter&quot;</span>&gt;0.5&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-minor-increment</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;<span class="kw">qti-major-increment</span> <span class="ot">unit=</span><span class="st">&quot;Meter&quot;</span>&gt;1.0&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-major-increment</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-rule-system-SI</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-rule</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-companion-materials-info</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="protractor" class="section">

##### 3.7.8.3 Protractor<a href="#h.1cden5c4t3aq" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.1cden5c4t3aq" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The test taker will be supplied with an on-screen protractor while
responding to the item. A human readable description can be included in
the description tag. Provide the measurement system using the increment
tag, which lets you provide a value for either the metric (radians) or
US (degrees) systems of angular measurement.

Example usage:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-companion-materials-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-protractor</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-description</span>&gt;A floating, transparent protractor that can be moved over the angles in the item.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-description</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-increment-US</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;<span class="kw">qti-minor-increment</span> <span class="ot">unit=</span><span class="st">&quot;Degree&quot;</span>&gt;1.0&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-minor-increment</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;<span class="kw">qti-major-increment</span> <span class="ot">unit=</span><span class="st">&quot;Degree&quot;</span>&gt;5.0&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-major-increment</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-increment-US</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-protractor</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-companion-materials-info</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="digital-materials" class="section">

##### 3.7.8.4 Digital Materials<a href="#h.pb4o2ebyhmje" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.pb4o2ebyhmje" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

These are content or reference materials that relate to the item
content. The materials can include some level of interactivity, but no
QTI interactions. Examples could be a map, a table of information, a
sheet of math formulas, an interactive periodic table of elements, or
even graphic creation tools. Use the \<qti-digital-material\> tag and
provide a link to the material by use of the qti-file-href tag.

An optional label and icon representing the digital material's
resource may be provided which the delivery system may use if it wants
to provide a link to the resource, or a button to launch the resource.

A MIME type may also be provided (via the attribute mime-type) which may
allow the delivery engine to optimize the delivery of the resource.

Example usage:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-companion-materials-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-digital-material</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;directory001/someDigitalFile.exe&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-digital-material</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">qti-digital-material</span> <span class="ot">label=</span><span class="st">&quot;The Periodic Table&quot;</span> <span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;application/pdf&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;directory001/tableOfTheElements.pdf&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-resource-icon</span>&gt;directory001/table.svg&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-resource-icon</span>&gt;   </span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-digital-material</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-companion-materials-info</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Digital Materials are intended to be presented to candidates during an
assessment session. However, these materials are meant to assist,
remind, guide, or in the case of a formative assessment, may even
instruct the candidate. The candidate's interaction with these materials
is not a requirement to responding to any assessment items, and no there
are no scoring implications for the use of the materials by candidates.

The reference to the materials from within the item is often done to
indicate that the particular reference materials are associated with the
item, and that the candidate should have access to the materials while
the assessment item is presented to the candidate. Because there is no
indication as to how the materials are to be presented to candidates,
the delivery system has discretion over the manner of presentation.
Assessment programs often provide direction as to their expectations
around the presentation of reference materials for candidates.

If candidates are required to access the materials to answer questions
(like a reading passage), use the assessment stimulus structures (see
[Section 3.7.7](#h.pb0clew9cko9) ) instead of digital materials. 3.7.8.5
 Physical Materials

These are external materials needed to work with, or respond with, when
the test taker responds to the item. Use the \<qti-physical-material\>
tag, then describe the materials using text.

Example usage:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-companion-materials-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-physical-material</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        Supply scissors and 2 sheets of 8.5 x 11 inch white paper.</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-physical-material</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-companion-materials-info</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

</div>

<div id="html5-accessibility-structures" class="section">

#### 3.7.9 HTML5 Accessibility Structures<a href="#h.zb7iu6ogdxw2" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.zb7iu6ogdxw2" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Within an QTI 3 item, content presented to candidates is largely
authored using HTML in the itemBody, qti-catalog-info, and
qti-modal-feedback nodes. Any prompts or interactions within the
itemBody use QTI 3 specific elements, which are commonly converted to
HTML elements at the time of delivery to the candidate. While authoring
QTI 3 content, and transforming any markup code to delivery code, it is
important to ensure that the content is presentable to as wide an
audience as possible. In addition to making content more accessible to
all candidates, using accepted web-delivery standards provides access to
candidates using assistive technology (AT).

Many of the recommendations around the techniques that provide
accessibility to candidates come from the World Wide Web Consortium's
(W3C) Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1. Implementers are
encouraged to use WCAG to provide guidance on making their assessments
accessible. The practices recommended below and throughout this document
are certainly not definitive, exhaustive, or complete. However, using
the native HTML structures will reduce the amount of special
accessibility markup required for assessment delivery.

Using semantically-rich markup aids AT because the content structure and
meaning can be programmatically determined. The WCAG 2.1 documentation
defines "programmatically determined" as:

determined by software from author-supplied data provided in a way that
different [user
agents](http://www.w3.org/TR/2008/REC-WCAG20-20081211/%23useragentdef),
including [assistive
technologies](http://www.w3.org/TR/2008/REC-WCAG20-20081211/%23atdef),
can extract and present this information to users in different
modalities.

There are several HTML elements that are also useful for accessibility
that can be used within an item, or an assessment interface. The
elements: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, figure, main, nav, aside, ul
(unordered list), ol (ordered list), table, footer, audio, video, p
(paragraph) - all provide semantic meaning to the content within the
element, and that meaning is used by assistive technology to be conveyed
to the user, as well as providing navigational assistance. Implementers
are encouraged to take advantage of native HTML elements to aid in
providing meaningful, understandable content to candidates.

<div id="use-of-headings-and-labels" class="section">

##### 3.7.9.1 Use of Headings and Labels<a href="#h.59zbeu9yirab" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.59zbeu9yirab" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

By using descriptive headings and labels in assessment items (and
assessment interfaces), the assessment experience for all candidates,
including Assistive Technology (AT) users, is substantially improved.
WCAG success criteria cover many of the aspects of using headings and
labels in web content. The success criteria for headings and labels
include:

- 2.4.6 Headings and Labels
- 2.4.10 Section Headings
- 3.2.4 Consistent Identification
- 3.3.2 Labels and Instructions
- 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value

In addition to headings providing useful descriptions of the content
that follows, they can provide structure to the content and allow AT
users to navigate through and around the content using keyboard
shortcuts. This allows the AT users to skim, read, and focus their
attention on the specific portions of the content that are important to
them, without the need of rereading unimportant information, and freeing
them from a content-linear user experience.

Heading levels should maintain their hierarchical order, as computer
programs would assume that heading levels with lower numbers are not
within heading sections with higher numbers.

Having labels for interactive components allows assistive technology
users (particularly screen reader users) to understand the purpose of
the interactive component, as well as identifying the current object why
the computer device has in focus. The use of WAI-ARIA to provide state
information (or for labelling custom created web components) is also
extremely helpful for AT users, and is discussed in more detail in
Section 2.4.1, as well as within many of the item examples (particularly
the transformation to delivery examples).

</div>

<div id="alt-text-and-long-descriptions" class="section">

##### 3.7.9.2 Alt Text and Long Descriptions<a href="#h.61xiqaidq9jl" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.61xiqaidq9jl" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Images (pictures, illustrations, graphs, charts, icons, etc.) intended
to provide meaningful content (non-decorative images) should include a
short description of the image. The length of the description can vary
from a few words to a couple of sentences. If the description needs to
exceed two or three sentences, or the description would benefit from
text formatting (use of lists, data tables, mathematical notation,
etc.), consider making a shorter description that identifies the image,
then add a long description for the image.

For assessments, the amount of information, and the specific information
provided, is particularly important. In some cases, over describing the
image can be a hindrance to understanding the image, or may add
unintended distractions for the candidate, so authors should include
only the amount of information that is important to the task, and for
which a sighted candidate might need to consider when responding to the
item.

Decorative images should include the empty alt text string (alt=""), as
this coding convention is used by AT to skip even mentioning the image
in any way. In some cases, assessments (particularly for younger
candidates) include images to increase the engagement of the assessment,
though the image is not critical to answer the question. Authors will
need to judge whether adding simple descriptions like "Picture of a
duck" is useful for the candidate. In many cases, treating these
engagement images as "decorative" may be appropriate.

If the image has a caption which is intended for all candidates
(regardless of accessibility requirements), consider using the HTML5
\<figure\> element which allows for a caption by using the
\<figcaption\> element. The \<figure\> element can also be useful when
displaying a series of images together. When providing multiple images
within a figure, the \<figcaption\> can be used to describe the grouping
of images collectively. If you use a caption for an image, you should
also include at least a short identifying description in the alt text
for any images within the figure.

For more complicated images, the simple description provided in the alt
text string may be inadequate to describe all the information within the
image, and writing a long string of text describing the image can be
difficult to navigate for AT users. In these cases, it is recommended
that a long description of the image be provided for candidates who
could benefit from them.

See Section 5.2.1.1 for additional information on alternative text, and
Section 5.2.1.2 for examples of long descriptions.

</div>

<div id="audio-and-video-tracks" class="section">

##### 3.7.9.3 Audio and Video Tracks<a href="#h.5198qxebtyrf" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.5198qxebtyrf" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The QTI 3 standard allows the use of the HTML5 elements audio and video.
These are useful not only because they provide semantic meaning for the
media object, but because they allow the use of tracks, which are used
to include alternative representations of the auditory and visual
aspects of the media.

To provide captions for a video object, use the "captions" vocabulary in
the "kind" attribute in the track element, as shown in the code snippet
below:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">video</span> <span class="ot">width=</span><span class="st">&quot;320&quot;</span> <span class="ot">height=</span><span class="st">&quot;240&quot;</span> <span class="ot">controls</span><span class="er">&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">source</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti3video.mp4&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;video/mp4&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">source</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti3video.ogg&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;video/ogg&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">track</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti3video_captions_en.vtt&quot;</span> <span class="ot">kind=</span><span class="st">&quot;captions&quot;</span> <span class="ot">srclang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">video</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The above example refers to a WebVTT file for the captions. Most
browsers have media players that automatically display a Closed Caption
(CC) icon in the controls bar if you supply a captions track, but check
your particular delivery implementation to ensure that candidates can
access the track. The media players then synchronize the captions
information supplied in the WebVTT file with the video playback.

Some video files include the captions in the video file itself.

While you can provide multiple languages as subtitles for videos, this
is typically not done for assessment content, as the captions provided
are expected to be in the language of the assessment content, and if the
content is also provided in a different language, that content is
provided in a completely different Assessment Item (QTI 3 refers to
these as item "variants").

There is a notable exception, where some assessments provide "stacked"
translations, where the content switches back and forth between two
languages by providing a chunk of content in one language (like a
paragraph of text), then following that chunk with the second language
translation. For stacked translated content, it would make sense to
include multiple languages, as well as providing the language in the
"srclang" attribute using a valid [\[BCP 47\]](#bib-BCP-47) language
tag, as well as using the "label" attribute of the track
(label="English") to identify the captions for the different language
versions. Typically, those language labels appear in a list when the
user selects the Closed Caption (CC) icon.

QTI 3 allows for the Media Fragments URI 1.0 notation (l [ink to W3C
documentation](https://www.w3.org/TR/media-frags/) ) as recommended by
the W3C as of 25 September, 2012. For example, if you wanted to play
only the first 20 seconds of a video file, you add the media fragment
notation to the end of the src URI, as shown in the example below:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">video</span> <span class="ot">width=</span><span class="st">&quot;320&quot;</span> <span class="ot">height=</span><span class="st">&quot;240&quot;</span> <span class="ot">controls</span><span class="er">&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">source</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;asl/item123_1.m4v#t=00:00:00,00:00:20.3&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;video/mp4&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">video</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

</div>

<div id="pnp-activated-content-and-the-use-of-catalogs-for-resource-storage"
class="section">

#### 3.7.10 PNP Activated Content and the Use of Catalogs for Resource Storage<a href="#h.m3ftkzwqnzhm" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.m3ftkzwqnzhm" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Within an QTI 3 Item, you can provide support-specific content through
the use of catalog resources. Catalog content in a QTI 3 Item is
considered "dormant" because it is only presented to candidates if their
PNP information or their program's business rules indicate that the
delivery system should provide them with the support-specific content
within the catalog.

It should be noted that general accessibility information should NOT be
supplied within catalogs, but rather should be included as content
readily available to all users using accepted web-based accessibility
markup practices. General accessibility information would include things
like alt text for images, captions for videos with audio, roles for
sections of content, headings, labels, and text descriptions for
non-text content.

An item can contain none, one, or many catalogs, all contained within
the qti-catalog-info node within an AssessmentItem file, inserted after
the qti-item-body content.

A single catalog can contain one or many supports, which are enumerated
using "cards" within the qti-catalog node. The "support" attribute of
the card describes which support is contained within the card. Each
catalog must only contain one of any of the particular supports. For
example, you cannot have a catalog with 2 cards with a
support="additional-directions". If the support is intended to match the
predefined supports (see [Section 3.7.10.1](#h.6976yfkh0soz)) of the
Personal Needs and Preferences 3.0, the support name in the support
attribute will exactly match the support name of the PNP. If the support
is *not* intended to match the predefined supports, then the support
name is considered "custom" and should follow the custom support
conventions (see [Section 3.7.10.3](#h.ev59heqa114l)).

If you need to further differentiate between candidates based on their
PNP needs and preferences, cards can contain one or more qti-card-entry
nodes, where the specific differentiated metadata is provided as an
attribute for each qti-card-entry. A common differentiating
characteristic is the language of the content for each qti-card-entry.

A qti-card or qti-card-entry may contain a single qti-html-content node,
or any number of qti-file-href nodes, which reference files outside the
item instance. A qti-card or qti-card-entry must contain at least some
content: either qti-html-content or a qti-file-href reference.

The qti-html-content node can contain any of the QTI 3 permitted HTML
elements, or plain text. The qti-html-content node cannot contain any
QTI 3-specific elements, including any interactions.

The qti-file-href nodes reference one or more external files that are
intended to be delivered for the specific support as listed in the
support attribute for the card or card-entry. When referencing multiple
files intended to provide the same support, use the mime-type attribute
to differentiate between the file types, where the delivery system
determines which file type is most appropriate to deliver in any
specific context. The multiplicity of files is not intended to provide
files for different user-based needs or preferences (as derived from the
candidate's PNP). If different content is needed for user-based needs,
the differences should be expressed in the attributes of a multiplicity
of card-entry instances for that support.

However the content with the qti-card or qti-card-entry is provided, the
delivery expectation is that the content for any specific support should
only be provided when requested. A request can come from either a
candidate's needs and preferences (their PNP), or from an administrative
request for all or some portion of the candidate pool; e.g., a testing
program could decide that ALL candidates should receive the
glossary-on-screen support, regardless of any specific requests from a
candidate's PNP. If no request for any particular support-specific
content is made, that content should remain dormant; i.e., not presented
to the candidate.

<div id="the-predefined-afa-3-0-supports-permitted-in-qti-3-item-content"
class="section">

##### 3.7.10.1 The Predefined Personal Needs and Preferences® 3.0 Supports Permitted in QTI 3 Item Content<a href="#h.6976yfkh0soz" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.6976yfkh0soz" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The QTI 3 profile of Personal Needs and Preferences 3.0 is a subset of
the full set of supports that are defined in Personal Needs and
Preferences 3.0. Additionally, only certain supports of the QTI 3
profile are allowed in the support attribute of cards, hereafter
referred to as the predefined supports. For many of the supports it is
not necessary to provide additional or alternative content in order to
provide a candidate with the support. Examples include supports like
breaks for candidates during testing, or providing a computer linereader
tool. The presentation/delivery system can provide these supports
without the need of authored information within the item. These supports
are therefore not needed in the context of authored content.

The predefined supports can be named in the support attribute without
the use of custom support extension. The IMS QTI 3 validator will check
and allow the use of the predefined supports.

The full list of predefined supports that can be authored in assessment
content includes:

- additional-directions
- audio-description
- braille
- glossary-on-screen
- high-contrast
- keyboard-directions
- keyword-translation
- linguistic-guidance
- long-description
- sign-language
- simplified-language-portions
- simplified-graphics
- spoken
- tactile
- transcript

</div>

<div id="how-to-reference-catalog-content" class="section">

##### 3.7.10.2 How to Reference Catalog Content<a href="#h.ke27wd3bu6ov" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.ke27wd3bu6ov" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Catalogs can be referenced from the qti-item-body element, from any
element within the qti-item-body node, and within any element within a
qti qti-catalog -> qti-card -> qti-html-content node.

A reference is made to the catalog using the attribute
"data-catalog-idref" from within an element in the qti-item-body - or on
the qti-item-body element itself - where the value of the attribute is
the id of the catalog you are referencing. A catalog *MUST* contain a
unique id in the qti-catalog element's start tag. In the simple item
example below, notice the reference (data-catalog-idref="catalog1") to
the qti-catalog on line 0019, where the data-catalog-idref value matches
the id in the qti-catalog element found on line 0029.

Note that the catalog is placed within the qti-catalog-info node
(starting on line 0028), and the qti-card supports the
"linguistic-guidance" feature (line 0029), as described within the
support attribute of the qti-card.

Example: Referencing Catalog Content

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">  https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ReferencingCatalogContent&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;Referencing Catalog Content&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en-US&quot;</span> <span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;multiple&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;QTI&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;AfA&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">min-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">max-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;3&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;prompt1&quot;</span>&gt;Indicate which of the following statements are </span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;catalog1&quot;</span>&gt;accurate.&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;QTI&quot;</span>&gt;QTI is an initialism for </span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        Question and Test Interoperability.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;PCI&quot;</span>&gt;PCI stands for Personal Care Information.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;AfA&quot;</span>&gt;AfA stands for Access for All.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;catalog1&quot;</span> &gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span>  <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;linguistic-guidance&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;Accurate means correct.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-34"><a href="#cb1-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-35"><a href="#cb1-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-36"><a href="#cb1-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span> <span class="ot">template=</span><span class="st">&quot;https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/rptemplates/match_correct&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-37"><a href="#cb1-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

A catalog can be referenced from more than one originating source. For
example, if there is additional content in the above example that also
used the word "accurate", you would point to the same catalog from all
the instances of the word accurate within the text.

Catalogs can contain more than one support, as shown in the slightly
more complex example below, where the catalog has support for both
keyword-translation and linguistic guidance.

The example also shows how a support with other user-based needs (as
provided in the candidate's PNP) uses multiple qti-card-entry nodes
where the attributes differentiate between which content is to be
presented to the candidate. In this example, the keyword-translations
are provided in multiple languages (the languages are specified using
[\[BCP 47\]](#bib-BCP-47) language tags), and the expectation is only
the language requested by the candidate in their PNP will be presented
to them.

Example: Catalog with Multiple Supports

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;CatalogWithMultipleSupports&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;Catalog with Multiple Supports&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en-US&quot;</span> <span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;multiple&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;QTI&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;AfA&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">min-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">max-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;3&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;prompt1&quot;</span>&gt;Indicate which of the following statements are </span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;catalog1&quot;</span>&gt;accurate.&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;QTI&quot;</span>&gt;QTI is an initialism for </span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          Question and Test Interoperability.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;PCI&quot;</span>&gt;PCI stands for Personal Care Information.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;AfA&quot;</span>&gt;AfA stands for Access for All.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;catalog1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;keyword-translation&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;es&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;preciso&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;de&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;genau&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-34"><a href="#cb1-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-35"><a href="#cb1-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-36"><a href="#cb1-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-37"><a href="#cb1-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;linguistic-guidance&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-38"><a href="#cb1-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;Accurate means correct.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-39"><a href="#cb1-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-40"><a href="#cb1-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-41"><a href="#cb1-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-42"><a href="#cb1-42" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-43"><a href="#cb1-43" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span> <span class="ot">template=</span><span class="st">&quot;https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/rptemplates/match_correct&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-44"><a href="#cb1-44" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

While an item may not contain any catalogs, it might also contain
multiple catalogs that support any number of pieces of content within
the item. In the example below, the sign-language support uses two
different catalogs to allow the stem and the response options to have
their own associations to portions of the sign language video file.

Example: Multiple Catalogs within a Single Item

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0 </span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_itemv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;MultipleCatalogs&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;Multiple Catalogs in a Single Item&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en-US&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;ChoiceA&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">div</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;stem&quot;</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;cat123_1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Grace walks 2 kilometres from her house on Maple Dr. to her harmonica lessons on Chestnut St.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">max-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;cat123_2&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;If Grace has lessons once a week, how many kilometres will she walk in a year for</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        her harmonica lessons?&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceA&quot;</span>&gt;About 200&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceB&quot;</span>&gt;About 100&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ChoiceC&quot;</span>&gt;About 50&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;cat123_1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;sign-language&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-34"><a href="#cb1-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;ase&quot;</span>  <span class="ot">default=</span><span class="st">&quot;true&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-35"><a href="#cb1-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-36"><a href="#cb1-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">video</span> <span class="ot">width=</span><span class="st">&quot;325&quot;</span> <span class="ot">height=</span><span class="st">&quot;183&quot;</span> <span class="ot">controls=</span><span class="st">&quot;controls&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-37"><a href="#cb1-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">source</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;asl/item123.mp4#t=00:00:01,00:00:10.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;video/mp4&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-38"><a href="#cb1-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;/<span class="kw">video</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-39"><a href="#cb1-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-40"><a href="#cb1-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-41"><a href="#cb1-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-42"><a href="#cb1-42" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-43"><a href="#cb1-43" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-44"><a href="#cb1-44" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;cat123_2&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-45"><a href="#cb1-45" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;sign-language&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-46"><a href="#cb1-46" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;ase&quot;</span>  <span class="ot">default=</span><span class="st">&quot;true&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-47"><a href="#cb1-47" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-48"><a href="#cb1-48" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">video</span> <span class="ot">width=</span><span class="st">&quot;325&quot;</span> <span class="ot">height=</span><span class="st">&quot;183&quot;</span> <span class="ot">controls=</span><span class="st">&quot;controls&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-49"><a href="#cb1-49" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">source</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;asl/item123.mp4#t=00:00:16.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;video/mp4&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-50"><a href="#cb1-50" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;/<span class="kw">video</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-51"><a href="#cb1-51" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-52"><a href="#cb1-52" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-53"><a href="#cb1-53" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-54"><a href="#cb1-54" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-55"><a href="#cb1-55" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-56"><a href="#cb1-56" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-57"><a href="#cb1-57" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

In addition to the primary qti-catalog-info node of an assessment item,
catalog content can be placed within the various structures that go
inside assessment items (i.e., rubric blocks, feedback structures,
template structures).

As a best practice, the scope of the catalog should be limited to the
container that holds (or could hold) a qti-catalog-info node. For
example, a rubric block within an assessment item's qti-item-body node
should reference catalogs within that rubric block. It should not jump
out of the rubric block and reference catalogs from the item's primary
qti-catalog-info node or other rubric blocks. Conversely, item content
outside the rubric block should never access catalogs inside the rubric
block's qti-catalog-info node. An example of the proper referencing is
shown below.

Example: Catalogs Across Content Nodes

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;CatalogsAcrossContentNodes&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;Catalogs Across Content Nodes&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en-US&quot;</span> <span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;multiple&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;QTI&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;AfA&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-rubric-block</span> <span class="ot">view=</span><span class="st">&quot;candidate&quot;</span> <span class="ot">use=</span><span class="st">&quot;instructions&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-content-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       &lt;<span class="kw">div</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;choice_directions&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;i123_rb1_cat1&quot;</span>&gt;Choose the option that best answers the question.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-content-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      </span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">&lt;!-- A rubric block contains its own qti-catalog-info node  --&gt;</span>     </span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;i123_rb1_cat1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;keyword-translation&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;es&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt; Elija la opcion que mejor responda la pregunta.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;de&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;Wahlen Sie die Option, die die Frage am besten beantwortet.</span>
<span id="cb1-34"><a href="#cb1-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-35"><a href="#cb1-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-36"><a href="#cb1-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-37"><a href="#cb1-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-38"><a href="#cb1-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-39"><a href="#cb1-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    </span>
<span id="cb1-40"><a href="#cb1-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-rubric-block</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-41"><a href="#cb1-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-42"><a href="#cb1-42" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">min-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">max-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;3&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-43"><a href="#cb1-43" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;prompt1&quot;</span>&gt;Indicate which of the following statements are </span>
<span id="cb1-44"><a href="#cb1-44" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;catalog1&quot;</span>&gt;accurate.&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-45"><a href="#cb1-45" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;QTI&quot;</span>&gt;QTI is an initialism for </span>
<span id="cb1-46"><a href="#cb1-46" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          Question and Test Interoperability.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-47"><a href="#cb1-47" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;PCI&quot;</span>&gt;PCI stands for Personal Care Information.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-48"><a href="#cb1-48" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;AfA&quot;</span>&gt;AfA stands for Access for All.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-49"><a href="#cb1-49" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-50"><a href="#cb1-50" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-51"><a href="#cb1-51" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-52"><a href="#cb1-52" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-53"><a href="#cb1-53" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;catalog1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-54"><a href="#cb1-54" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;keyword-translation&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-55"><a href="#cb1-55" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;es&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-56"><a href="#cb1-56" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;preciso&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-57"><a href="#cb1-57" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-58"><a href="#cb1-58" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;de&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-59"><a href="#cb1-59" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;genau&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-60"><a href="#cb1-60" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-61"><a href="#cb1-61" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-62"><a href="#cb1-62" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-63"><a href="#cb1-63" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-64"><a href="#cb1-64" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span> <span class="ot">template=</span><span class="st">&quot;https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/rptemplates/match_correct&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-65"><a href="#cb1-65" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
