</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

For interoperability purposes, catalog ids within assessment content
files (items, sections, stimulus, parts/tests) *MUST* be unique. Be
aware that there may be referenced/included/nested content (like a
shared stimulus or rubric blocks) that may also include catalogs and
could be on the same delivery presentation screen for candidates. It is
the expectation that Authoring systems ensure there are no catalog
naming conflicts across structures when exported.

</div>

<div id="custom-supports" class="section">

##### 3.7.10.3 Custom Supports<a href="#h.ev59heqa114l" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.ev59heqa114l" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Cards within a catalog can also contain custom content - content for
supports other than the predefined supports allowed in QTI 3 (see
Section 3.7.10.1 for the list).

To include your custom content, use the prefix "ext:" in the name of the
support attribute on the card. As a best practice, your custom name
should include the name of the organization implementing the custom
support, in order to avoid namespace collisions with other organizations
that may create similar custom supports. In the example below, the
fictional organization "KXZ Testing" will include "kxztesting" in the
custom support name (line 0012), which follows the lowercase & hyphen
naming convention used throughout QTI 3 and Personal Needs and
Preferences® 3.0. The IMS QTI 3 validator will allow any alphabetical
characters (a-z, A-Z, 0-9) (plus dash and underscore) following the
extension prefix. It will not check for any spelling consistencies for
custom-created support names.

Example: Labelling Custom Support Content

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  ...</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  ...</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;prompt1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    Why did Max cross the ocean in his &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;cat0001&quot;</span>&gt;dirigible&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;?</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  ...</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;cat0001&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;ext:kxztesting-illustrated-glossary-on-screen&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               &lt;<span class="kw">h2</span>&gt;Term: Dirigible&lt;/<span class="kw">h2</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               &lt;<span class="kw">img</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;images/dirigible.svg&quot;</span> <span class="ot">alt=</span><span class="st">&quot;&quot;</span> <span class="ot">width=</span><span class="st">&quot;128&quot;</span> <span class="ot">height=</span><span class="st">&quot;128&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;il-gloss-def&quot;</span>&gt;A lighter-than-air aircraft, capable of navigating</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  through the air using its own power. These aircraft are usually in an elongated balloon</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  shape, with the passenger compartment located on the bottom side of the aircraft.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

If the custom support is intended to be delivered for specific
candidates based on their PNP information, the PNP should also use the
Personal Needs and Preferences 3.0 extension mechanism, using the same
name of the custom support used in the content. (See Section 5.4)

</div>

</div>

<div id="context-declarations" class="section">

#### 3.7.11 Context Declarations<a href="#h.7tgz0gr02037" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.7tgz0gr02037" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

When evaluating Template Processing and Response Processing instructions
in an Assessment Item, having contextual information available in the
information model can improve the effectiveness and efficiency of such
Template Processing and Response Processing instructions.  For example,
Template Processing and Response Processing instructions with easy
access to a candidate's PNP "context" can yield richer, more effective,
presentation and outcomes. In another example, enabling an evaluation
system to pass system or environmental "context" into an Assessment Item
- or an Assessment Test - using common information model conventions can
add richness to item evaluation and improve interoperability.

The built-in QTI_CONTEXT record variable can be visualized with the
following qti-field-identifier structure when declared in a QTI3
Assessment Item:

The key elements to qti-context-declaration are:

1.  A  variable declaration which declares a variable with global scope.
    Such a globally scoped variable can be accessed within
    \<qti-template-processing\>, \<qti-response-processing\>, and
    \<qti-outcome-processing\> instructions. This approach uses the
     element qti-context-declaration.

A \<qti-context-declaration\> has all of the typical attributes; e.g.,
cardinality, identifier, and base-type.

2.  A built-in \<qti-context-declaration\> for a variable named
    "QTI_CONTEXT", with cardinality of record. The following is an
    explicit (though unnecessary) \<qti-context-declaration\> of
    QTI_CONTEXT:

\<qti-context-declaration cardinality="record" identifier="QTI_CONTEXT"
/\>

"QTI_CONTEXT" is the name used in order to avoid collisions with other
variables that might already be declared in existing items.

3.   Systems that support context declarations are *required* to support
    three field definitions within the QTI_CONTEXT record:

<!-- -->

1.  **candidateIdentifier**: a string used to map to a candidate object.
    A practical application of this would be to encode a user or session
    ID which could then be used as the key to additional user/candidate
    information
2.  **testIdentifier**: a string used to map to a test object. A
    practical application of this would be to encode an
    \<qti-assessment-test\> identifier which could then be used as the
    key to additional "test" contextual information.
3.  **environmentIdentifier**: a string used to map to an environment
    runtime in which template processing or response processing is being
    evaluated. A practical application of this would be to encode a
    "program" identifier which could then be used as the key to
    additional system-level properties.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co">&lt;!-- QTI_CONTEXT is built-in. The following is an example of an explicit declaration --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-context-declaration</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;record&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;QTI_CONTEXT&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          <span class="co">&lt;!-- the following three fields MUST be implemented by a conforming system --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span> <span class="ot">field-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;candidateIdentifier&quot;</span>&gt;Curly&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span> <span class="ot">field-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;testIdentifier&quot;</span>&gt;essay-test&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span> <span class="ot">field-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;environmentIdentifier&quot;</span>&gt;2&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            <span class="co">&lt;!-- other, optionalexample fields in this QTI_CONTEXT record --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span> <span class="ot">field-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;optionalField1&quot;</span>&gt;Larry,Moe,Curly&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;integer&quot;</span> <span class="ot">field-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;optionalField2&quot;</span>&gt;3&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-default-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-context-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     ...</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt; ... &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    ...</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

A system supporting context declarations must implement the
candidateIdentifier, testIdentifier, and environmentIdentifier fields of
the QTI_CONTEXT record.

Note: A common expected usage of QTI_CONTEXT is to enable more
interoperable methods of retrieving a candidate's PNP information. For
example, PNP information can be located by using the candidateIdentifier
field as a key, alone, or in conjunction with the testIdentifier field.

In the next example, different programs use one item to capture and
evaluate essays. However, each program has chosen to implement different
partial scoring ratios. An environmentIdentifier is passed into an item
using the QTI_CONTEXT environmentIdentifier field (lines 39-41). This
value (either "1" or "2" in this example) is then used with a
\<qti-match-table\> (lines 12-15) to find the partial scaling factor for
a program.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0 </span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;contexts_for_different_program_scoring&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;Contexts for different partial scoring across programs&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">adaptive=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span> <span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">&lt;!-- QTI_CONTEXT is declared here, even though it is built into the information model. --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-context-declaration</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;record&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;QTI_CONTEXT&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;UNSCALED_RAW_SCORE&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">&lt;!-- Lookup table to map an environment to a scaling factor --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;PROGRAM_SCALING_FACTOR&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-match-table</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-match-table-entry</span> <span class="ot">source-value=</span><span class="st">&quot;1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">target-value=</span><span class="st">&quot;0.5&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-match-table-entry</span> <span class="ot">source-value=</span><span class="st">&quot;2&quot;</span> <span class="ot">target-value=</span><span class="st">&quot;0.6&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-match-table</span>&gt;  </span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Describe pros and cons of electronic personal interactions as opposed to face-to-face?&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-extended-text-interaction</span> <span class="ot">expected-length=</span><span class="st">&quot;1000&quot;</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">&lt;!-- Assign a raw, unscaled score for the essay --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;UNSCALED_RAW_SCORE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-field-value</span> <span class="ot">field-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;overall&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-34"><a href="#cb1-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-35"><a href="#cb1-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">&lt;!-- ScoreEssay returns a record with 6 fields, including an &quot;overall&quot; field for the raw essay score --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-36"><a href="#cb1-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-custom-operator</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;example.customOperators.ScoreEssay&quot;</span> <span class="ot">definition=</span><span class="st">&quot;essay-rubric-1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-37"><a href="#cb1-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-variable</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-38"><a href="#cb1-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-custom-operator</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-39"><a href="#cb1-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-field-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-40"><a href="#cb1-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-41"><a href="#cb1-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-42"><a href="#cb1-42" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co">&lt;!-- Now, scale the score using the environmental information passed into the item --&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-43"><a href="#cb1-43" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-44"><a href="#cb1-44" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-product</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-45"><a href="#cb1-45" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-custom-operator</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;example.customOperators.stringToNumber&quot;</span> <span class="ot">definition=</span><span class="st">&quot;string-to-number&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-46"><a href="#cb1-46" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-lookup-outcome-value</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;PROGRAM_SCALING_FACTOR&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-47"><a href="#cb1-47" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">qti-field-value</span> <span class="ot">field-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;environmentIdentifier&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-48"><a href="#cb1-48" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">qti-variable</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;QTI_CONTEXT&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-49"><a href="#cb1-49" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-field-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-50"><a href="#cb1-50" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-lookup-outcome-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-51"><a href="#cb1-51" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-custom-operator</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-52"><a href="#cb1-52" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-variable</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;UNSCALED_RAW_SCORE&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-53"><a href="#cb1-53" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-product</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-54"><a href="#cb1-54" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-set-outcome-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-55"><a href="#cb1-55" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-processing</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-56"><a href="#cb1-56" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="portable-custom-interactions-pcis" class="section">

#### 3.7.12 Portable Custom Interactions (PCIs)<a href="#h.jsncxx6a57ao" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.jsncxx6a57ao" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

As discussed in [Section 3.2.23](#h.98xaka8g51za) , PCIs can be used to
build interoperable interactions with custom user interface and
behaviour.

The functionality of a PCI is implemented by an AMD module written in
Javascript, which may load any other AMD modules upon which it depends.

PCIs are introduced into an item by the use of a
*qti-portable-custom-interaction* element. As with the predefined
interaction types, PCIs are bound to a response variable via the
response-identifier attribute.

As two different qti-portable-custom-interaction elements could
represent two very different custom interaction types, a PCI *MUST* have
an attribute (custom-interaction-type-identifier) to identify the actual
interaction type represented by this PCI. To prevent name collisions
between the values used by different implementers it is *RECOMMENDED*
that a Federated Content URN
\[<a href="#bib-rfc4198" class="bibref" data-link-type="biblio"
title="A Uniform Resource Name (URN) Namespace for Federated Content">rfc4198</a>\] be
used for the value of the custom-interaction-type-identifier attribute.

Any custom attributes provided by the item author through the use of
data- attributes on the qti-portable-custom-interaction element *MUST*
be passed as properties by the delivery engine to the PCI module on
initialization.

The item author *MAY* pass the values of template variables defined in
the item to the PCI module via qti-template-variable child elements.

The item author *MAY* pass HTML markup to the PCI module to customize
the user interface of the PCI via a qti-interaction-markup child
element. It is the responsibility of the PCI module to decide what use
(if any) it will make of this markup. The markup allowed would be
similar to that allowed by a qti-rubric-block, so it could for example
include qti-printed-variable or inline feedback elements. One difference
with qti-rubric-block is that the qti-interaction-markup element *MAY*
contain a *template*element which in turn may contain any HTML5 markup.

</div>

<div id="pci-amd-default-module-resolution" class="section">

##### 3.7.12.1PCI AMD Default Module Resolution<a href="#h.rrz6g7fej3px" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.rrz6g7fej3px" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

As the delivery engine must dynamically load the AMD module which
implements the PCI module and any AMD module that it depends on, it
needs to know how to resolve references to AMD module names to loadable
Javascript files.

PCI supports loading AMD modules from global URLs (e.g. CDN (content
delivery network) hosted Javascript files) and also from QTI content
package relative files. As a QTI content package may be deployed in more
than one context (e.g. it may be used in a normal web browser with
access to external CDN hosted files or it may be used in a high stakes
secure browser context where access is permitted only to files included
in the QTI content package) PCI allows both a primary location
(typically used to reference CDN hosted files) and a fallback location
(typically referencing content package provided files) to be used when
specifying AMD module locations.

The first mechanism supported for AMD module resolution is to provide a
module resolution configuration file called "module_resolution.js" in
the QTI content package which defines the item in a directory called
"modules".

This file defines the primary location of each named AMD module.

This *MUST* be supported by delivery engines. While it is expected that
this will be the most common way to resolve the AMD modules used by PCIs
its use by content authors and QTI content packages is *RECOMMENDED*,
but not mandatory.

</div>

<div id="pci-amd-module-resolution-file-format" class="section">

##### 3.7.12.2 PCI AMD Module Resolution File Format<a href="#h.y9qi2anwp78o" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.y9qi2anwp78o" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

This config file is a JSON document like the following example:

<figure id="fig-todo-caption-0" class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs json"><code class="sourceCode json"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">{</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="er"> </span> <span class="dt">&quot;waitSeconds&quot;</span><span class="fu">:</span> <span class="dv">15</span><span class="fu">,</span>  <span class="er"> </span><span class="dt">&quot;paths&quot;</span><span class="fu">:</span> <span class="fu">{</span><span class="er">        </span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="dt">&quot;module1&quot;</span><span class="fu">:</span> <span class="st">&quot;https://example.com/js/modules/graph1.01/graph&quot;</span><span class="fu">,</span><span class="er">        </span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="dt">&quot;module2&quot;</span><span class="fu">:</span> <span class="st">&quot;https://foobar.com/foo/bar1.2/foo&quot;</span> <span class="er"> </span><span class="fu">}}</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span></code></pre></div>
<figcaption>Figure 107 <span class="fig-title"> @@@ TODO caption
</span></figcaption>
</figure>

The "waitSeconds" property defines how long the delivery engine should
wait for each Javascript file to load before timing out. A setting of 0
indicates that no timeout should be used (however this may prevent a
fallback configuration from being used). If "waitSeconds" is not
specified then a default of 7 seconds should be assumed.

The paths object provides a collection of AMD module name to URL
mappings. The path supplied should not contain a file extension.

If the location starts with a protocol (e.g. https://) then the module
should be fetched from that location. Otherwise it should be assumed to
be a QTI content package relative path.

A delivery engine *MAY* ignore any package relative locations which are
not defined in the manifest of the QTI content package.

A delivery engine *MAY* apply security rules to the URLs from which it
will load Javascript files.

In the example above if an AMD require call was made for "module1" the
delivery engine would first read the module resolution configuration
from "\<content package base href\>/modules/module_resolution.js" and
then load the Javascript for the module from
<https://example.com/js/modules/graph1.01/graph.js>

</div>

<div id="pci-amd-default-fallback-module-resolution" class="section">

##### 3.7.12.3 PCI AMD Default Fallback Module Resolution<a href="#h.ey9qb5kwxt4v" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.ey9qb5kwxt4v" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The second module resolution mechanism is to provide a module resolution
configuration file called "fallback_module_resolution.js" in the QTI
content package which defines the item in a directory called "modules".

This *MUST* be supported by delivery engines, but its use in QTI content
packages is optional. For any modules where the primary location is not
a package relative path it is *RECOMMENDED* that a fallback location is
configured which uses a package relative path.

The file format and processing rules are the same as defined in section
3.7.12.2 above, but the locations provided are those to be used if a
timeout or a fatal error (such as a 403 error) is encountered when
attempting to load the module from the primary location.

</div>

<div id="associating-a-pci-module-to-the-interaction" class="section">

##### 3.7.12.4 Associating a PCI module to the interaction<a href="#h.kcjqymxla265" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.kcjqymxla265" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

To associate a PCI module with a qti-portable-custom-interaction (PCI),
either the "module" attribute or a qti-interaction-modules element
*MUST* be present.  The value of the "module" attribute (or the
qti-interaction-modules element) *MUST* be the name of an AMD module
defined in the module resolution configuration.

If we take the example of a simple PCI which implements a custom Likert
Scale interaction where the QTI content package included a module
resolution configuration in "modules/module_resolution.js" with the
contents: `{`

<figure id="fig-todo-caption-1" class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs css"><code class="sourceCode css"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &quot;paths&quot;<span class="in">:</span> {        </span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &quot;exampleLikertScale&quot;<span class="ch">:</span> <span class="st">&quot;modules/likert&quot;</span><span class="op">,</span>        </span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="st">&quot;handlebars&quot;</span>: <span class="st">&quot;modules/lib/handlebars.min&quot;</span>    }}</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span></code></pre></div>
<figcaption>Figure 108 <span class="fig-title"> @@@ TODO caption
</span></figcaption>
</figure>

Then the item author can use:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;likertPCI&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;PCI example&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en&quot;</span> &gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;How well do you think you have mastered the concepts introduced in this module?&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-portable-custom-interaction</span></span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="ot">module=</span><span class="st">&quot;exampleLikertScale&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="ot">custom-interaction-type-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;urn:fdc:example.com:202308:pci:likertScale&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="ot">data-label-min=</span><span class="st">&quot;Very Poorly&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="ot">data-label-max=</span><span class="st">&quot;Very Well&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-orientation-horizontal&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-interaction-markup</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-portable-custom-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

When this item is rendered by the delivery engine it will read the
"module" attribute's value ("exampleLikertScale") and after looking this
module name up in "modules/module_resolution.js" will attempt to
dynamically load "modules/likert.js" from the QTI content package. If
the AMD module in likert.js required a module called "handlebars", then
the location for that will be resolved in the same way and the delivery
engine will attempt to dynamically load "modules/lib/handlebars.min.js"
from the QTI content package.

The item author *MAY* also override the default location for the primary
resolution config files by adding a qti-interaction-modules child
element with a "primary-configuration" attribute to set the location for
the primary module resolution configuration and *MAY* set the fallback
module resolution configuration location by setting a
"fallback-configuration" attribute. This *MUST* be supported by the
delivery engine, however the delivery engine *MAY* refuse to load
relative paths which are not defined in the content package's manifest,
and *MAY* apply security rules to the URLs it will load Javascript from.

The item author *MAY* further customize the module loading for an
interaction by specifying the primary and fallback locations to use for
an individual module by adding one or more qti-interaction-module child
elements to the qti-interaction-modules element. This *MUST* be
supported by the delivery engine.

For example, there may be no default module resolution configuration
files in the content package, but there may be a module resolution file
called e.g. modules/maths_config.js with the contents:
`{   "waitSeconds": 10,   "paths": {        "exampleLikertScale": "modules/likert",        "exampleGraphing": "modules/graphing",        "handlebars": "modules/lib/handlebarsv101.min"   }}`

Assuming that both PCI modules require a module called "handlebars" but
require different versions of the handlebars module, the item author may
wish to override the module version in the item definition, e.g.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;graphingPCI&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;Equation graphing example&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en&quot;</span> <span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;string&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;How well do you think you have mastered the concepts introduced in this module?&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-portable-custom-interaction</span></span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="ot">module=</span><span class="st">&quot;exampleGraphing&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="ot">custom-interaction-type-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;urn:fdc:example.com:2023:pci:graphing&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="ot">data-label-min=</span><span class="st">&quot;Very Poorly&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="ot">data-label-max=</span><span class="st">&quot;Very Well&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-orientation-horizontal&quot;</span> &gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-interaction-modules</span> <span class="ot">primary-configuration=</span><span class="st">&quot;modules/maths_config.js&quot;</span> &gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">qti-interaction-module</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;handlebars&quot;</span> <span class="ot">primary-path=</span><span class="st">&quot;modules/lib/handlebarsV2.min&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-interaction-modules</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-interaction-markup</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-portable-custom-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="example-pci-lifecycle" class="section">

##### 3.7.12.5 Example PCI lifecycle<a href="#h.vf2yl2nwwnu" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.vf2yl2nwwnu" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Depending on the item definition, on the set of features implemented by
the delivery engine, and on the portable custom interaction, there are
many possible PCI lifecycles.

The first example flow is of a non-adaptive, non-composite item
containing a portable custom interaction. Here the candidate navigates
away from the item while it is unsubmitted, and then later returns to
complete the item and submit it. The delivery engine stores the state of
the item it retrieves from the PCI object in the item session and when
the candidate returns to the item it passes that stored state back to
the custom interaction instance.

<figure id="fig-pci-lifecycle-example-1">
<img src="/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl3image39.png"
style="width: 624.00px; height: 925.33px; margin-left: 0.00px; margin-top: 0.00px; transform: rotate(0.00rad) translateZ(0px); -webkit-transform: rotate(0.00rad) translateZ(0px);"
alt="Image showing the interaction of Candidate, Delivery Engine, Communication Bridge, and Custom Interaction within the PCI Lifecycle." />
<figcaption>Figure 109 <span class="fig-title"> PCI Lifecycle example 1.
</span></figcaption>
</figure>

In this second example a more complex flow is shown where a candidate
may interact with an adaptive composite item which may contain more than
one portable custom interaction and a qti-end-attempt-interaction across
multiple attempts where the candidate uses the endAttemptInteraction to
end each attempt and a button in the delivery engine to 'submit' the
item, the item response processing then closes the item.

<figure id="fig-pci-lifecycle-example-2">
<img src="/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl3image41.png"
style="width: 624.00px; height: 809.33px; margin-left: 0.00px; margin-top: 0.00px; transform: rotate(0.00rad) translateZ(0px); -webkit-transform: rotate(0.00rad) translateZ(0px);"
alt="Image showing the interaction of Candidate, Delivery Engine, Communication Bridge, and Custom Interaction within the PCI Lifecycle." />
<figcaption>Figure 110 <span class="fig-title"> PCI Lifecycle example 2.
</span></figcaption>
</figure>

</div>

<div id="pci-api" class="section">

3.7.12.6 PCI
API<a href="#h.u2afaj6oprj9" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.u2afaj6oprj9" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Any Javascript loaded as part of or to support a PCI *MUST* be written
to run inside a strict Javascript execution context (e.g. it must expect
that the delivery engine has called "use strict" before any of its
modules are loaded) \[STRICT\]. It is recommended that all PCI modules
also call "use strict".

</div>

<div id="pci-communication-bridge-methods" class="section">

##### 3.7.7 PCI Communication Bridge Methods<a href="#h.ibcksu8902cr" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.ibcksu8902cr" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The delivery engine supports portable custom interactions by injecting a
Javascript object which supports the PCI Communication Bridge contract
into the loaded PCI module via AMD. This object allows PCIs to register
themselves and exchange state and configuration settings with the
delivery engine. The delivery engine will provide this via a standard
AMD definition of 'qtiCustomInteractionContext' which the portable
custom interaction can require in its AMD module.As an alternate method
of discovering the qtiCustomInteractionContext object, the delivery
engine *MUST* also assign it to the global window object before the PCI
is loaded.

When a delivery engine is rendering an item which contains one or more
portable custom interaction instances, it needs a way to obtain the
factory interface needed to create new PCI objects to implement the
portable custom interaction instances in the item. The key which links
the two is that each qti-portable-custom-interaction element has a
custom-interaction-type-identifier attribute which is a string that
uniquely identifies the factory that will be used to create PCI objects
of the type needed by that qti-portable-custom-interaction instance.
That instance also identifies the module(s) which are required to be
loaded for that PCI.

As these modules are loaded they must ensure that one will call the
*register*method of the bridge object to register the factory for that
PCI with the Communication Bridge under a key which is the
typeIdentifier of the PCI module.

The delivery engine can then later call the getInstance method of the
Communication bridge to cause it to render itself to the DOM element
passed in the call. The delivery engine will also pass the value of the
custom-interaction-type-identifier attribute of the
qti-portable-custom-interaction it is rendering as the first parameter
of the call, and the Communication Bridge will then look up the factory
the PCI registered with that typeIdentifier and then call getInstance on
that factory, proxying on the other parameters provided. The getInstance
call returns a reference to the object thus created.

The qti-interaction-markup element of the PCI may contain HTML which in
turn may contain QTI elements such as qti-variable etc. It is the
responsibility of the delivery engine to resolve those elements. As this
HTML is passed to the PCI when getInstance is called, the PCI can then
choose to modify that HTML as it sees fit. Consequently, the delivery
engine should resolve these elements before generating the HTML in the
DOM element passed to the PCI, and whenever the rendering of that HTML
might change; e.g., after response or outcome processing where response
and outcome variables used in that HTML might change, the delivery
engine should update the HTML and call getInstance on the PCI again to
pass it the updated HTML.

The *register* method will be called once per PCI type, in each AMD
loading context where the PCI module is loaded.  How an implementation
defines AMD loading contexts is beyond the scope of this document.  For
example, there may be only one loading context for an entire assessment,
or loading contexts for each item, or for each portable custom
interaction instance, depending on the implementation.

If a delivery engine supports composite interactions the
qtiCustomInteractionContext getInstance method may be called multiple
times to construct more than one PCI in the Assessment Item.

Before unloading a PCI, if the PCI object provides an oncompleted
callback function the delivery engine *MUST* call it to allow the PCI to
release any global resources it allocated (e.g. global event listeners).

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th colspan="3"><strong>PCI Communication Bridge
(qtiCustomInteractionContext) Required Methods</strong></th>
<th></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td colspan="3"><p><strong>register(customInteraction)</strong></p>
<p>After the PCI module loads it uses this method to register its
customInteraction factory object which provides a factory method used to
create PCI instances</p></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><strong>getInstance(typeIdentifier, dom, configuration,
state)</strong></td>
<td>The typeIdentifier argument should be the value of the
typeIdentifier property of the customInteraction factory object passed
in a previous call to the <em>register</em>method. It should also match
the value of the <em>custom-interaction-type-identifier</em>on the
<em>qti-portable-custom-interaction</em> element.The other arguments of
this call are the same as the arguments of the
<em>getInstance</em>method of the registered custom interaction for the
PCI and are documented in the next section.Returns the object
created.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<div id="pci-api-pci-module-factory" class="section">

###### 3.7.12.7.1 PCI API - PCI Module Factory<a href="#h.1mc9puik2ft6" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.1mc9puik2ft6" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th colspan="3"><strong>PCI Communication Bridge - PCI
Factory</strong></th>
<th></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td colspan="3"><p><strong>getInstance(dom, configuration,
state)</strong></p>
<p>This is the registered PCI module factory method for constructing a
new instance of the custom interaction with the supplied configuration
and state.</p>
<p>The <em>dom</em>parameter is required. It is a reference to the DOM
element where the delivery engine has transformed and injected the
<em>qti-interaction-markup</em> child of the
<em>qti-portable-custom-interaction</em> into the HTML DOM.</p>
<p>The <em>state</em> parameter is optional. It can be passed to
recreate a previous interaction state of the item. The value of state,
if provided, should have been previously returned by the same
interaction type.</p>
<p>It returns the object created.</p>
<p>The object passed as the required <em>configuration</em>parameter
should contain the following properties:</p></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><em>properties</em></td>
<td colspan="2">An object containing all of the key/value pairs from the
data- attributes set on the qti-portable-custom-interaction.The naming
rules applied by HTML5 for element.dataset should be followed here.  For
example, the data- is removed and hyphenated names have the hyphens
stripped and are camelCased.  Also, data-max-choices-message="Too Many"
would become properties: { maxChoicesMessage: "Too Many" }</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><em>templateVariables</em></td>
<td colspan="2">An object containing all of the template variables
referenced (via qti-template-variable elements) in the
qti-portable-custom-interaction and their current values.The values of
variables <em>MUST</em> follow the structure defined in Appendix C.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><em>contextVariables</em></td>
<td colspan="2">An object containing all of the context variables
referenced (via qti-context-variable elements) in the
qti-portable-custom-interaction and their current values. The values of
variables <em>MUST</em> follow the structure defined in Appendix C.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><em>boundTo</em></td>
<td colspan="2">The value of the response variable of this
qti-portable-custom-interaction.The values of variables <em>MUST</em>
follow the structure defined in Appendix C</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><em>responseIdentifier</em></td>
<td colspan="2">The interaction's response identifier; e.g., "RESPONSE".
 Useful for logging and events. This should not be relied on to be
unique for the web-page the interaction is rendered on ( e.g. multiple
items may be rendered on the same web page).</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><em>onready</em></td>
<td colspan="2">Callback method for the PCI instance to call when it is
fully constructed and ready for interaction.The signature of this
callback function is documented in the next Section.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><em>ondone</em></td>
<td colspan="2"><em>OPTIONAL</em>. Callback method which <em>MAY</em> be
called by the PCI module if the candidate indicates that they have
finished interacting with the item; e.g., by clicking on a button
rendered by the PCI. The custom interaction can identify that the item
state should change; e.g. the attempt should be ended, or that a model
solution be shown to the candidate.The signature of this callback
function is documented in the next Section.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><em>status</em></td>
<td colspan="2"><p><em>OPTIONAL</em>. Specify the item status (as per
Section 4.1 of the QTI 3 Information Model). If not specified it should
default to "interacting".</p>
<p>The values of status which may be passed to a PCI are:</p>
<ul>
<li>interacting</li>
<li>suspended</li>
<li>closed</li>
<li>solution</li>
<li>review</li>
</ul></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="pci-communication-bridge-api-delivery-engine-callbacks"
class="section">

###### 3.7.12.7.2 PCI Communication Bridge API - Delivery Engine Callbacks<a href="#h.lopy3gknkieb" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.lopy3gknkieb" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

When the delivery calls *getInstance,* the *configuration*object
contains one required and one optional callback, *onready*and *ondone*,
respectively.  The PCI instance is responsible for calling these
callback functions on the events described below.

These two callbacks are defined below:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 33%" />
<col style="width: 33%" />
<col style="width: 33%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th colspan="2"><strong>onready</strong></th>
<th></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p>This callback <em>MUST</em> be called by the custom
interaction instance once it has finished initialization and is ready
for the candidate to interact with.  </p>
<p>When <em>onready</em> is called, the following two parameters below
<em>MUST</em> be provided.</p></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><em>interaction</em></td>
<td>The custom interaction instance (as created by a call to
getInstance)</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><em>state</em></td>
<td>The current internal state of the custom interaction instance. This
should be treated as an opaque string by the delivery engine. It can be
passed back by the delivery engine in a getInstance call to recreate the
PCI in the same state.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 33%" />
<col style="width: 33%" />
<col style="width: 33%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th colspan="2"><strong>ondone</strong></th>
<th></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p>This callback <em>MAY</em> be called by the custom
interaction instance if the candidate indicates that they have finished
interacting with the item (e.g. by clicking on a button rendered by the
PCI).   For example, it might be used to request that the item be
closed, or that the item transition to "solution" state.</p>
<p>When <em>ondone</em> is called, the first three parameters below
<em>MUST</em> be provided.</p></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>interaction</td>
<td>The custom interaction instance (as created by a call to
getInstance)</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>response</td>
<td>The response variable to which this qti-portable-custom-interaction
is bound and its value. The values of variables <em>MUST</em> follow the
structure defined in Appendix C.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>state</td>
<td><p>The current internal state of the custom interaction instance.
This should be treated as an opaque string by the delivery engine.</p>
<p>It can be passed back by the delivery engine to the PCI module in a
getInstance call to recreate the PCI in the same state.</p></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>status</td>
<td><p><em>OPTIONAL</em>. Requests that the delivery engine transition
the item to the specified state. If not specified, it should default to
"interacting". For example, if a value of "closed" is provided that
would indicate that the item state should be set as closed, which would
also end the attempt. The delivery engine is not required to transition
the item state as requested.</p>
<p>The values of status that a delivery engine should expect a PCI to
send are:</p>
<ul>
<li>interacting</li>
<li>closed</li>
<li>solution</li>
<li>review</li>
</ul></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="pci-api-pci-object" class="section">

##### 3.7.19.3 PCI API - PCI Object<a href="#h.o4nb5dpg6ica" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.o4nb5dpg6ica" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The PCI module *getInstance*factory method returns an instance of the
portable custom interaction.  Each instance will correspond to one
occurrence of *qti-portable-custom-interaction* in an assessment item.
PCI objects have the following properties and methods.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 50%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th colspan="2"><strong>PCI Instance</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p><strong>getResponse()</strong></p>
<p>This method may be called zero or more times. The type of the
response data must correspond with the base-type and cardinality defined
in the QTI response declaration of the response variable the
qti-portable-custom-interaction is bound to (via the response-identifier
attribute).</p>
<p>The value returned must follow the format defined in Appendix C.  If
the user has not yet interacted with the interaction, or if the response
is not valid for any reason, then getResponse should return
<em>undefined</em>.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p><strong>getState()</strong></p>
<p>This method can be used to retrieve the current state of the custom
interaction instance. This can be later used to re-initialize the PCI to
that state. This may, for example, be used by delivery engines when a
student navigates away from the current item to another item and later
returns to resume their interaction with the item. This should be
treated as an opaque string by the delivery engine.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><strong>oncompleted</strong><em>OPTIONAL</em>. The
custom object instance <em>MAY</em> implement an oncompleted callback
function. If a function has been assigned to the oncompleted property of
the PCI object, then this callback function <em>MUST</em> be called by
the delivery engine before unloading the PCI module, or before removing
markup associated with the PCI from the DOM.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p><strong>typeIdentifier</strong></p>
<p>This should have the same value as the
custom-interaction-type-identifier attribute in the
qti-portable-custom-interaction element.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p><strong>checkValidity()</strong></p>
<p><em>OPTIONAL</em>. Check whether the candidate's interaction with the
PCI satisfies any validity constraints defined for this PCI instance;
i.e., is the interaction's response valid?</p>
<p>If the validity constraints are not met, the PCI <em>MUST</em> fire a
cancelable <em>invalid</em> event at elements containing invalid input,
and then return false.  If the validity constraints are met, the PCI
<em>MUST</em> return true.  If this PCI has no validity constraints, or
the PCI can not yet validate the candidate's input, the PCI
<em>MUST</em> return <em>undefined</em>.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p><strong>reportValidity()</strong></p>
<p><em>OPTIONAL</em>. Behave exactly as checkValidity(), except if false
is returned the PCI <em>SHOULD</em> also report validation problems
identified to the candidate.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p><strong>setCustomValidity()</strong></p>
<p><em>OPTIONAL</em>. Set the custom validity message for the PCI to the
specified message. Use the empty string to indicate that the element
does not have a custom validity error.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p><strong>getCustomValidity()</strong></p>
<p><em>OPTIONAL</em>. Return the custom validity message for the PCI.
Use the empty string to indicate that the element does not have a custom
validity error.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The custom interaction instance is responsible for calling the callback
functions set by the delivery engine in the getInstance call via the
onready and/or ondone properties of the configuration. See [Section
3.7.12.9](#h.lopy3gknkieb)  for the expected signature of these
functions, and for information on when in the interaction life cycle the
custom interaction instance should call these callback functions.

</div>

<div id="pci-communication-bridge-api-pci-callback" class="section">

##### 3.7.19.4 PCI Communication Bridge API - PCI callback<a href="#h.u7b7rqmr68xl" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.u7b7rqmr68xl" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The custom interaction instance returned by the PCI module *MAY*
implement an "oncompleted" callback which *MUST* be called by the
delivery engine before unloading the PCI module or removing markup
associated with the PCI from the DOM.

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th colspan="2"><strong>oncompleted()</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><em>oncompleted</em>has no parameters. If provided, this
callback <em>MUST</em> be called by the delivery engine before unloading
the PCI module or removing markup associated with the PCI from the DOM.
 This is to allow the custom interaction instance to release any
allocated resources; e.g., unbind global event listeners etc., or remove
markup, which might not be deallocated or removed automatically by
removing the DOM element associated with the PCI. Typically, the
delivery engine will call <em>getResponse</em>and
<em>getState</em>before calling <em>oncompleted.</em></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="notifying-changes-to-the-delivery-engine" class="section">

##### 3.7.19.5 Notifying Changes to the Delivery Engine<a href="#h.j7ycey3vcdlh" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.j7ycey3vcdlh" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

When the value of a PCI changes (e.g. the value which would be returned
by getResponse() changes) a PCI *MUST* send a qti-interaction-changed
event by firing a custom event to the DOM element passed to getInstance.
 The PCI can determine when it considers the response value to have
changed.  For example, a PCI does not have to send a
qti-interaction-changed event on every keypress or mouse click - unless
of course the designer of the PCI explicitly , it does not have to e.g.
send an event on every keypress.   When sent, the custom event *MUST* be
set to bubble and be cancellable. The custom event *MUST* be initialized
with the following data ( which will appear as a 'detail' object on the
event ).

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th colspan="3"><strong>qti-interaction-changed</strong></th>
<th></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>interaction</td>
<td colspan="2">The PCI object <em>MUST</em> provide itself. It is
recommended that the bridge/delivery engine always use this value to
resolve which qti-portable-custom-interaction the change relates
to.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>responseIdentifier</td>
<td colspan="2">The PCI <em>MUST</em> provide the value of
responseIdentifier provided in the getInstance call. This value should
not be relied on to be a unique identifier on the web page.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>valid</td>
<td colspan="2">The PCI <em>SHOULD</em> provide a boolean which provides
the value that a call to checkValidity() would return; i.e., whether or
not the PCI's response meets the PCI's validity constraints.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>value</td>
<td colspan="2">The PCI <em>SHOULD</em> provide the value that a call to
getResponse() would return.</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="configuration-of-a-pci" class="section">

##### 3.7.19.6 Configuration of a PCI<a href="#h.rzkohh35d3bs" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.rzkohh35d3bs" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Configuration of portable custom interactions may utilize data-
attributes of the qti-portable-custom-interaction. The configuration
data is passed to the PCI module via a call to *getInstance*.

QTI template variables may also be utilized to provide configuration
data to custom interactions by template variable binding. The delivery
system must read the mapping and provide configuration data before
interaction initialization. QTI template variables are transcribed into
JSON data by the delivery system using the JSON binding defined in
Appendix C.

PCI module developers should consider that a PCI module may be used in a
composite item when deciding how to bind template variables to a PCI as
template variables are defined at the level of the item. For template
variables which the PCI module expects to be given a unique value for
each interaction in the item the PCI module author should consider
approaches to providing the item author flexibility over how the
template variables can be named so that they can be unique to the
instance but still recognized by the PCI module.

Some techniques which the PCI module author might consider applying are

- Allowing a suffix to be applied to the template variable name; e.g.,
  where a template variable is used to provide a range on a number line
  and which will be mapped to an internal variable called *range*then
  any template variable starting with *'range'*which the item author has
  bound to the PCI instance via a qti-template-variable element would be
  mapped to the internal variable *'range',*so the item author could use
  any of the following template variable names, *range*, *rangeOne*,
  *rangeTwo*, *rangeline1*.
- Allow a suffix to be applied as above but require the item author to
  provide the value of the suffix as a documented data attribute on the
  qti-portable-custom-interaction element; e.g.,using data-instance-id="
  *One*" might then cause the PCI module to expect the template variable
  to be named " *rangeOne*". This suffix might apply to multiple
  template variables used by the PCI module.
- Allow the item author to override a default expectation for the
  template variable name via a data attribute on the
  qti-portable-custom-interaction element; e.g.,
  data-range-variable-name=" *rangeOne*" would cause the PCI Module to
  map a template variable called " *rangeOne*" rather than defaulting to
  mapping a template variable called " *range*" to an internal variable
  " *range*".

Where the values of the template variables are not expected to be unique
to an instance, PCI module authors should consider selecting template
variables which are not likely to be the same as the names required by
other PCI modules which might be used in the same item, e.g. by using a
vendor prefix.

The JSON data is passed into the custom interaction object on creation
via the getInstance method. The example item in [Section
3.8.2](#h.z1bzfyi60fsm)  below binds two template variables (X and Y) to
this interaction, so that the value of these two variables will be
passed by the delivery engine into the configuration in the
templateVariables property when the custom interaction object is
created.

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div id="annotated-item-examples" class="section">

### 3.8 Annotated Item Examples<a href="#h.bun0cxx70owp" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.bun0cxx70owp" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

<div id="example-1-implementing-shared-vocabulary-aria-and-catalogs-with-glossary-and-keyword-translation"
class="section">

#### 3.8.1 Example 1 - Implementing Shared Vocabulary, ARIA, and Catalogs with Glossary and Keyword Translation<a href="#h.7x7ukes4f72u" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.7x7ukes4f72u" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

This example demonstrates a variety of techniques involving QTI3 shared
CSS vocabulary, shared interaction vocabulary, use of aria for users of
assistive technology, and Catalogs for encoding glossaries, keyword
translations, and computer read aloud.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 50%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:m=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0  </span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd </span></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML </span></span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/mathml/v3p0/schema/xsd/mathml3.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti3-choice-aria-mathml-catalog&quot;</span> <span class="ot">title=</span><span class="st">&quot;Surveyors&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">adaptive=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span> <span class="ot">time-dependent=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en&quot;</span> &gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;identifier&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;ChoiceA&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-value</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-correct-response</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-response-declaration</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-outcome-declaration</span> <span class="ot">base-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;float&quot;</span> <span class="ot">cardinality=</span><span class="st">&quot;single&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SCORE&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">div</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-layout-row&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">div</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-layout-col6&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">h2</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-visually-hidden&quot;</span>&gt;Passage&lt;/<span class="kw">h2</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;cat01&quot;</span>&gt;Examine&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt; the following survey map.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
