##### 5.2.1.7 Spoken: link-indication<a href="#h.hyayne86gq5u" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.hyayne86gq5u" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The link-indication parameter allows for the following vocabulary:

- speak-link
- different-voice
- sound-effect
- none

The example below shows the assignment to the spoken support, a
screen-reader reading-type, and a number of preferences related to TTS
technology.

**Spoken, Preferences PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">spoken</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">reading-type</span>&gt;screen-reader&lt;/<span class="kw">reading-type</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">typing-echo</span>&gt;words&lt;/<span class="kw">typing-echo</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">speech-rate</span>&gt;240&lt;/<span class="kw">speech-rate</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">pitch</span>&gt;0.7&lt;/<span class="kw">pitch</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">volume</span>&gt;0.7&lt;/<span class="kw">volume</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">link-indication</span>&gt;different-voice&lt;/<span class="kw">link-indication</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">spoken</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">additional-testing-time</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">unlimited</span>/&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">additional-testing-time</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="pronunciation-guidance-for-spoken-candidates" class="section">

##### 5.2.1.8 Pronunciation Guidance for Spoken Candidates<a href="#h.z4qhy3rjhr9m" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.z4qhy3rjhr9m" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

While TTS engines continue to improve, some words or characters can
present problems in the rendering of the pronunciation of spoken text.
Some users can be confused by the differences in meaning of words when
the pronunciation isn't precise. There are a number of different
standards that allow the control of pronunciation of text-based content.
The QTI 3 standard allows for 2 different standards, including:

1.  SSML 1.1 (Speech Synthesis Markup Language)
    \[<a href="#bib-speech-synthesis11" class="bibref" data-link-type="biblio"
    title="Speech Synthesis Markup Language (SSML) Version 1.1">speech-synthesis11</a>\]
2.  PLS (Pronunciation Lexicon Specification)
    \[<a href="#bib-pronunciation-lexicon" class="bibref"
    data-link-type="biblio"
    title="Pronunciation Lexicon Specification (PLS) Version 1.0">pronunciation-lexicon</a>\]

SSML markup can be used directly into the default content markup, and
the pronunciation is specific to the exact location of the markup in the
particular assessment item that employs the SSML markup. SSML can be
used to indicate specific pronunciations, the location and length of
pauses, volume, pitch, rate, etc. across different synthesis-capable
platforms.

At the time of the release of this Best Practices and Implementation
Guide, there is some uncertainty about the best practice of how to
markup pronunciation information within HTML content. Browsers and
text-to-speech engines may adopt markup methods that use the same kind
of vocabulary used in SSML versions 1.0 and 1.1, but may not use the
namespaced XML markup that is allowed in version 3 of QTI.

The mostly widely adopted (currently) of the pronunciation methods is
SSML, and QTI 3 allows for the use of SSML directly in the item default
content using inline markup or namespaced SSML markup.

One approach to inserting SSML into HTML content employs JSON markup for
the SSML data. By using a "data-ssml" attribute, the SSML data can be
included in the delivery markup without transformation, and can still be
used by text-to-speech engines.

For full documentation of this approach, see the [1EdTech Support for
Speech Synthesis Markup Language (SSML) Using the 'data-ssml'
Property](https://www.imsglobal.org/sites/default/files/spec/qti/v3/data-ssml_infobind/imsqtiv3p0_datassml_v1p0_InfoBind.html)
document which contains the Information Model, JSON Binding and Best
Practices. An example of the approach is shown below.

**Item Example with Inline SSML Pronunciation using JSON markup**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0   </span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   identifier=&quot;inline-pronunciation&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    title=&quot;In which, SSML and JSON is used inline for pronunciation.&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    adaptive=&quot;false&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xml:lang=&quot;en-US&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>... </span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;content2&quot;</span>&gt;Answer the following question.</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">data-ssml=</span><span class="st">&#39;{&quot;break&quot;:{&quot;time&quot;:&quot;2s&quot;}}&#39;</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span> <span class="ot">maxChoices=</span><span class="st">&quot;1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">min-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;content1&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;content6&quot;</span>&gt;Sigmund </span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">data-ssml=</span><span class="st">&#39;{&quot;sub&quot;:{&quot;alias&quot;:&quot;Froyd&quot;}}&#39;</span>&gt;Freud&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt; and </span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  Carl &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">data-ssml=</span><span class="st">&#39;{&quot;sub&quot;:{&quot;alias&quot;:&quot;young&quot;}}&#39;</span>&gt;Jung&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt; both belong to the </span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">data-ssml=</span><span class="st">&#39;{&quot;emphasis&quot;:{&quot;level&quot;:&quot;strong&quot;}}&#39;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">data-ssml=</span><span class="st">&#39;{&quot;sub&quot;:{&quot;alias&quot;:&quot;sigh-co-analytical&quot;}}&#39;</span>&gt;psychoanalytical</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt; school of psychology.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;content8&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;true&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;content7&quot;</span>&gt;True&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;content4&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;content3&quot;</span>&gt;False&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The following example demonstrates the namespaced SSML approach. For
complete documentation on the use of SSML 1.1, see the W3C, and the
documentation for SSML 1.0
\[<a href="#bib-speech-synthesis11" class="bibref" data-link-type="biblio"
title="Speech Synthesis Markup Language (SSML) Version 1.1">speech-synthesis11</a>\].

**Item Example with Inline Pronunciation Using Namespaced SSML**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:ssml=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0   </span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd</span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/ssml/v1p1/schema/xsd/ssmlv1p1-core.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   identifier=&quot;inline-pronunciation&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    title=&quot;In which, SSML is used inline for pronunciation.&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    adaptive=&quot;false&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xml:lang=&quot;en-US&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>... </span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;content2&quot;</span>&gt;Answer the following question.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span> <span class="ot">maxChoices=</span><span class="st">&quot;1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">min-choices=</span><span class="st">&quot;0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;content1&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;content6&quot;</span>&gt;Sigmund &lt;<span class="kw">ssml:sub</span> <span class="ot">alias=</span><span class="st">&quot;Froyd&quot;</span>&gt;Freud&lt;/<span class="kw">ssml:sub</span>&gt; and Carl </span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">ssml:sub</span> <span class="ot">alias=</span><span class="st">&quot;young&quot;</span>&gt;Jung&lt;/<span class="kw">ssml:sub</span>&gt; both belong to the &lt;<span class="kw">ssml:sub</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">alias=</span><span class="st">&quot;sigh-co-analytical&quot;</span>&gt;psychoanalytical&lt;/<span class="kw">ssml:sub</span>&gt;  school of psychology. </span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">fixed=</span><span class="st">&quot;true&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;true&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;content7&quot;</span>&gt;True&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span> <span class="ot">fixed=</span><span class="st">&quot;true&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;false&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;content3&quot;</span>&gt;False&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-simple-choice</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-choice-interaction</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

To use a common shared pronunciation lexicon across your assessment
content, you can include Pronunciation Lexicon Standard (PLS) files in
the content package. Note that not all TTS engines support the PLS
standard, and exporting systems including PLS files in their content
package should communicate with delivery systems about the use of PLS.

The pronunciation lexicon may be used in conjunction with the SSML
markup, where SSML is used for local pronunciation hints, and the PLS
lexicon is used for shared pronunciation problems. Using the shared
lexicon can dramatically reduce the amount of SSML markup required by
content authors, because authors need only to adjust pronunciation for
context or unique pronunciation problems.

An example of how to reference a PLS file is shown below. See the W3C
documentation on the PLS standard
\[<a href="#bib-pronunciation-lexicon" class="bibref"
data-link-type="biblio"
title="Pronunciation Lexicon Specification (PLS) Version 1.0">pronunciation-lexicon</a>\]
for creating valid PLS files.

The PLS method allows for the reference of files that list pronunciation
rules to be employed when reading the assessment content. These
pronunciation files are referenced from within a test package.

**QTI 3 Example of a Content Package Referencing a PLS file**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">manifest</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;QTI 3.0 PLS Packaging Example&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">metadata</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">schema</span>&gt;QTI Item&lt;/<span class="kw">schema</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">schemaversion</span>&gt;3.0.0&lt;/<span class="kw">schemaversion</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">metadata</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">organizations</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">resources</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">resource</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;imsqti_item_xmlv3p0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;Resource1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;qtiv3p0_01.xml&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">file</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;qtiv3p0_CoreTest_VC_IP_01.xml&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">dependency</span> <span class="ot">identifierref=</span><span class="st">&quot;SoundResource&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;<span class="kw">dependency</span> <span class="ot">identifierref=</span><span class="st">&quot;PLS1&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">resource</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">resource</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;pls&quot;</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;plsfile.pls&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;PLS1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">file</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;plsfile.pls &quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">resource</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">resource</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;associatedcontent/learning-application-resource&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;Sound.mp3&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;SoundResource&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">file</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;Sound.mp3&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">resource</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">resources</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">manifest</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

In the PLS example below, the use of PLS for the correct pronunciation
of 'Drosophila Melanogaster' is demonstrated. The content of the
corresponding PLS file for this example:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">lexicon</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">alphabet=</span><span class="st">&quot;ipa&quot;</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;en-us&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">lexeme</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                &lt;<span class="kw">grapheme</span>&gt;drosophila&lt;/<span class="kw">grapheme</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                &lt;<span class="kw">phoneme</span>&gt;dro?&#39;s?f?l?&lt;/<span class="kw">phoneme</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">lexeme</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">lexeme</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                &lt;<span class="kw">grapheme</span>&gt;melanogaster&lt;/<span class="kw">grapheme</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                &lt;<span class="kw">phoneme</span>&gt;m?&#39;li¿½n??gi¿½st?r&lt;/<span class="kw">phoneme</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">lexeme</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">lexicon</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

See Section 6 for more information on Content Packaging for QTI 3.

</div>

<div id="text-to-speech-suppression" class="section">

##### 5.2.1.9 Text-to-Speech Suppression<a href="#h.yxb99q1qil14" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.yxb99q1qil14" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

To suppress text-to-speech (i.e., to hide content from assistive
technology like screen readers) the accepted web practice and the QTI 3
recommended practice is to use the aria-hidden="true" attribute in the
element you are trying to hide and prevent that content from being read
aloud.

There are unique use cases in assessment contexts where you (at the
direction of assessment programs) may wish to suppress text-to-speech
for specific kinds of text-to-speech users, which Personal Needs and
Preferences 3.0 refers to as the spoken support. In QTI 3, if you wish
to suppress some content from being read aloud to candidates, use the
special "data-qti-suppress-tts" attribute and use the standardized QTI 3
vocabulary (computer-read-aloud, screen-reader, all), which correlates
to the spoken reading-type vocabulary.

Note that no existing screen readers natively support these attributes,
and delivery engines will likely need to make specific delivery code
modifications to enable this capability to work as intended. The QTI
shared vocabulary is the interoperable method for expressing the concept
of differentiated text-to-speech suppression.

Below is an example of using the text-to-speech (TTS) suppression
attribute to suppress the labels being read aloud for a screen-reader
candidate. The label information is only useful for candidates who can
see where the text is associated with graphic. The computer-read-aloud
candidate typically only has visible text read aloud to them, so we
could suppress the read aloud of the long description.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="er">...</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Below is a figure with &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;inline-math&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">data-qti-suppress-tts=</span><span class="st">&quot;computer-read-aloud&quot;</span>&gt;12&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt; boxes.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">div</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;figure1-with-labels&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">img</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;figure1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">height=</span><span class="st">&quot;165&quot;</span> <span class="ot">width=</span><span class="st">&quot;250&quot;</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;figure1.svg&quot;</span> <span class="ot">alt=</span><span class="st">&quot;Figure showing </span></span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">  Rectangle ABCD divided into 12 equal area boxes. 4 of the boxes are shaded.&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">aria-describedby=</span><span class="st">&quot;long-description-figure-1&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;labelA&quot;</span> <span class="ot">data-qti-suppress-tts=</span><span class="st">&quot;screen-reader&quot;</span>&gt;Point A&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;labelB&quot;</span> <span class="ot">data-qti-suppress-tts=</span><span class="st">&quot;screen-reader&quot;</span>&gt;Point B&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;labelC&quot;</span> <span class="ot">data-qti-suppress-tts=</span><span class="st">&quot;screen-reader&quot;</span>&gt;Point C&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;labelD&quot;</span> <span class="ot">data-qti-suppress-tts=</span><span class="st">&quot;screen-reader&quot;</span>&gt;Point D&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">div</span> <span class="ot">id=</span> <span class="st">&quot;long-description-figure-1&quot;</span> <span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-visually-hidden long-description&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">data-qti-suppress-tts=</span><span class="st">&quot;computer-read-aloud&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Rectangle ABCD...the long description goes here...&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">div</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;In the figure above, what fraction of the rectangle &lt;<span class="kw">em</span>&gt;ABCD&lt;/<span class="kw">em</span>&gt; is </span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>shaded?&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="visually-hide-content" class="section">

##### 5.1.1.6.10 Visually Hide Content<a href="#h.ipk8xrn8ywtv" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.ipk8xrn8ywtv" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

There are some occasions where you wish to make content programmatically
available to assistive technology, but not visible to candidates. While
an implementation would likely use CSS to accomplish the visual
presentation, the practice in QTI 3 is to identify this visually-hidden
content using the "**qti-visually-hidden**" class. This class has the
opposite intent of the aria-hidden="true" attribute used to visually
show content but hide it from assistive technology.

</div>

</div>

<div id="braille" class="section">

#### 5.2.2 Braille<a href="#h.wga3darsqm5t" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.wga3darsqm5t" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The default assumption in a QTI PNP is that braille will be available to
the candidate via a refreshable braille display. To explicitly affirm
the use of a refreshable braille display, use the delivery-mode
parameter with the "refreshable" vocabulary. To indicate the candidate
wishes to receive embossed paper-based braille, the PNP should use the
"embossed" vocabulary as the delivery-mode. Note that the availability
of embossed braille materials generally involves advanced planning to
ensure the proper accessibility materials are available for the
candidate.

Refreshable braille displays use the same delivery content markup
provided for screen readers, which should be provided in the delivery
markup code by default. Generally, screen reader software does the
braille transcription work, based on the settings and preferences
established by the user. The PNP is also capable of storing and
transferring the braille preferences for the candidate using these
additional optional parameters:

1.  grade
2.  braille-type
3.  math-type
4.  lang

The **grade** parameter has three possible values: 1, 2, or 3. Grade 1
braille indicates that the braille is uncontracted (no letter
combinations). Grade 2 indicates the braille is contracted, and this is
the default value. If no grade value is provided, the expectation is
that the braille is grade 2. Grade 3 braille has an even greater level
of contraction. Note that in some cases, the higher level of contraction
is not available, in which case the grade 2 should be provided to the
candidate.

The **braille-type** parameter indicates the kind of braille the
candidate is requesting. The available values are "ebae" (English
Braille American Edition) and "ueb" (Universal English Braille). To
indicate a different braille-type than the predefined values, use the
"ext:" prefix and provide the name of the braille type. Delivery systems
will need to be made aware of the specific braille-type used in the
braille-type extension to be able to provide the braille-type for
candidates. Note it may be simpler to use the "lang" attribute to
indicate a particular country's braille conventions where there are no
ambiguities about the braille types.

The **math-type** parameter indicates the preferred braille math
encoding. The available values are "nemeth" (Nemeth) and "ueb"
(Universal English Braille). To indicate a different math-type than the
predefined math-type values, use the "ext:" prefix and provide the name
of the math-type. Delivery systems will need to be made aware of the
specific math-type used in the math-type extension to be able to provide
"ext:" math-types for candidates.

The **lang** parameter would indicate a specific language requirement of
the candidate. Use the [\[BCP 47\]](#bib-BCP-47) notation to specify the
language. As a best practice, implementers should use the two-letter
language codes (with variant modifiers) of ISO 639-1 when available, and
use the three-letter codes of ISO 639-2 and ISO 639-3 when the language
is not available as a 2-letter code.

There is a known issue of presenting inline SSML pronunciation strings
to braille users, where the substituted pronunciation string of
characters is sent to the refreshable braille display instead of the
default text characters. This occurs because the refreshable braille
display uses the content feed from the screen reader software that is
driving the braille display. The preferred delivery would be for the
candidate to receive the actual, correct spelling, as provided in the
default content, and not in the SSML pronunciation features. At the time
of the writing of this documentation, there is no known workaround for
this issue.

Some assessment programs create braille encoded files that are
associated with assessment content. Candidates are given the option to
access those files, either via their refreshable braille display, or
printing out the file to use simultaneously with the electronically
delivery, or separately. An example of associating a set of braille
files for the entire content of an item is shown below

**Braille File References for Different Kinds of Braille**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="er">...</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;thx113eight&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Some content...<span class="er">&lt;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;thx113eight&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;braille&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">data-braille-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;ueb&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> <span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;text/plain&quot;</span>&gt;braille/ueb/thx1138_ueb.brf&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">data-braille-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;ebae&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> <span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;text/plain&quot;</span>&gt;braille/ebae/thx1138_ebae.brf&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

**QTI 3 Example of a Content Package Referencing a Braille Ready File**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">manifest</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;QTI_3_0_BRF_Packaging_Example&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">metadata</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">schema</span>&gt;QTI Item&lt;/<span class="kw">schema</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">schemaversion</span>&gt;3.0.0&lt;/<span class="kw">schemaversion</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">metadata</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">organizations</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">resources</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">resource</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;imsqti_item_xmlv3p0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;Resource1&quot;</span> <span class="er"> </span>                    </span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                  href=&quot;qtiv3p0_CoreTest_VC_IP_02.xml&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">file</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;qtiv3p0_CoreTest_VC_IP_01.xml&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">dependency</span> <span class="ot">identifierref=</span><span class="st">&quot;SoundResource&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;<span class="kw">dependency</span> <span class="ot">identifierref=</span><span class="st">&quot;thx1138_ueb&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;<span class="kw">dependency</span> <span class="ot">identifierref=</span><span class="st">&quot;thx1138_ebae&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">resource</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">resource</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;associatedcontent/learning-application-resource&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>                 href=&quot;braille/ebae/thx1138_ebae.brf&quot; identifier=&quot;thx1138_ebae&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">file</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;braille/ebae/thx1138_ebae.brf&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">resource</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">resource</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;associatedcontent/learning-application-resource&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>                 href=&quot;braille/ebae/thx1138_ueb.brf&quot; identifier=&quot;thx1138_ueb&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">file</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;braille/ebae/thx1138_ueb.brf&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">resource</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">resource</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;associatedcontent/learning-application-resource&quot;</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;Sound.mp3&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>                 identifier=&quot;SoundResource&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">file</span> <span class="ot">href=</span><span class="st">&quot;Sound.mp3&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">resource</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">resources</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">manifest</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="input-requirements" class="section">

#### 5.2.3 Input Requirements<a href="#h.i4xxpj5laobu" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.i4xxpj5laobu" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

By default, QTI 3 delivery interfaces should allow for keyboard
navigation and operation. The input-requirements support in Personal
Needs and Preferences 3.0 allows for PNPs to make explicit input
requirement statements, as well as other input devices (mouse, switch,
touchscreen). Note that there are some keyboard exceptions, so if the
intent is to ensure a candidate can only use a keyboard, they may need
to be assigned to known forms or items that fully support keyboard
access. See also Section 5.2.5.3 (input requirements).

</div>

<div id="additional-testing-time" class="section">

#### 5.2.4 Additional Testing Time<a href="#h.ew17cznhalye" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.ew17cznhalye" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Some candidates, particularly those who have accessibility and
accommodation needs, require additional time to complete their
assessment. Personal Needs and Preferences 3.0 has a number of different
methods for indicating the amount of extra time, including the following
parameters:

- time-multiplier
- fixed-minutes
- unlimited

The default assignment is "unlimited", and is assigned as shown below.
It indicates that the candidate can choose when they feel they have
completed the assessment.

**Additional Testing Time (unlimited) PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">additional-testing-time</span>&gt;     </span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">unlimited</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">additional-testing-time</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

If you wish to indicate a percentage of extra testing time, use the
time-multiplier parameter, where the number used multiplies the amount
of the original testing time. This allows the testing time to change
based on the amount of testing time for a particular test. For example,
if the candidate is to be given one and a half the amount of testing
time, and the original testing time of a particular test is 60 minutes,
you would put the number 1.5 to indicate the candidate should have 90
minutes of testing time (as shown in the example below). Or if a
different test has a 30 minute restriction, the candidate would have 45
minutes to complete the test.

**Additional Testing Time (time-multiplier) PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">additional-testing-time</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">time-multiplier</span>&gt;1.5&lt;/<span class="kw">time-multiplier</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">additional-testing-time</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

If you wish to indicate a fixed amount of extra testing time, use the
fixed-minutes parameter, where the number used expresses the amount of
extra time in minutes. For example, if the candidate is to be given 30
minutes of extra testing time, and the original testing time is 60
minutes, you would put the number 30 to indicate the candidate should
have 90 minutes of testing time (as shown in the example below). Or if a
different test has a 30 minute restriction, the candidate would have 60
minutes to complete the test.

**Additional Testing Time (fixed-minutes) PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">additional-testing-time</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">fixed-minutes</span>&gt;30&lt;/<span class="kw">fixed-minutes</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">additional-testing-time</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

**Assessments with One or More TestPart(s)**

In the case of an assessment with one or more TestPart(s), you can
specify the additional time to each part. For example, if the assessment
has two parts, both limited to 30 minutes each, you may apply 30 minutes
of additional testing to each part for a total of 60 minutes. If you
scope the time to the assessment level, the time will be evenly split
between the parts. How the time is split is at the discretion of the
delivery system. There may be cases where parts have unequal amounts of
effort, and the additional time may divide proportionally.

Many assessments have interface controls that display the remaining
amount of testing time. When a candidate has an additional testing time
requirement, the displayed time should reflect the additional, or
unlimited amount of time for the candidate.

</div>

<div id="hazard-avoidance" class="section">

#### 5.2.5 Hazard Avoidance<a href="#h.knsolcmlz6rd" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.knsolcmlz6rd" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Some candidates have particular sensitivities to specific kinds of
stimuli. Personal Needs and Preferences has a support called
"hazard-avoidance" which permits the following vocabulary:

- flashing
- olfactory-hazard
- sound
- motion-simulation

Candidates who have indicated any of the above sensory issues should not
be exposed to content that contains that particular kind of stimuli.

**Hazard Avoidance PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">hazard-avoidance</span>&gt;flashing&lt;/<span class="kw">hazard-avoidance</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

</div>

<div id="language-preferences" class="section">

#### 5.2.6 Language Preferences<a href="#h.p6aygdf32l2y" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.p6aygdf32l2y" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

There are a number of supports within Personal Needs and Preferences
which have language preferences. The sections below detail how to
specify those language preferences in a candidate's PNP, how the content
should be prepared to transfer the content, and for any delivery
expectations.

<div id="item-translation" class="section">

##### 5.2.6.1 Item Translation<a href="#h.zfz0rwq2av4j" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.zfz0rwq2av4j" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Item translation is used for a candidate to request that the language of
the assessment content be delivered in the language specified, using the
ISO-639 standard. For assessment programs to provide the requested
language, the program needs to plan and prepare the assessment content
in the program approved language. Most assessment registration systems
carefully coordinate the languages that can be requested to the
languages available for the assessment. In QTI 3, you can create a
language variant of the item when the item content is completely
translated into an additional language. See the Content Packaging
(Section 6) for details on how to include variants in a package.

In the example below, the candidate has indicated that their preferred
language is Korean (the
<a href="#bib-BCP-47" class="bibref" data-link-type="biblio">[BCP
47]</a> primary language tag for Korean is "ko", which is supplied in
the required lang attribute.

**Item Translation PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">item-translation</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;ko&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="keyword-translation" class="section">

##### 5.2.6.2 Keyword Translation<a href="#h.r32hgvrww7qj" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.r32hgvrww7qj" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Instead of, or in addition to, supplying full translation of assessment
content (item-translation), some assessment programs elect to provide
translations of certain keywords within the content. Only those keywords
are permitted, or are capable of providing the translation into the
languages designated by the program.

To assign a candidate to the keyword-translation support, and to
designate the specific language the candidate should be presented, use
the "keyword-translation" support (line 7 in the example below), with a
valid <a href="#bib-BCP-47" class="bibref" data-link-type="biblio">[BCP
47]</a> language tag in the lang attribute.

**Keyword Translation PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">keyword-translation</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;es&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The content example below shows the support for the word accurate (line
6) with two different translations. Because there are likely a
multiplicity of languages to be provided, you don't put the translation
content directly in the keyword-translation card, rather, the different
languages are stored in different qti-card-entry nodes within card that
supports keyword-translation. In this example, pre-recorded sound files
of the translated words are provided within the qti-html-content (lines
16, 17, 25, and 26).

**Keyword-Translation Item Example**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="er">...</span> xml:lang=&quot;en&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;prompt1&quot;</span>&gt;Indicate which of the following statements are &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;ae029&quot;</span>&gt;accurate.&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;ae029&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;keyword-translation&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;es&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;preciso&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">audio</span> <span class="ot">controls=</span><span class="st">&quot;controls&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                  &lt;<span class="kw">source</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;translations/es/preciso.ogg&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;audio/ogg&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                  &lt;<span class="kw">source</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;translations/es/preciso.mp3&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;audio/mpeg&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;/<span class="kw">audio</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;de&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;genau&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;<span class="kw">audio</span> <span class="ot">controls=</span><span class="st">&quot;controls&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                  &lt;<span class="kw">source</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;translations/de/genau.ogg&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;audio/ogg&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                  &lt;<span class="kw">source</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;translations/de/genau.mp3&quot;</span> <span class="ot">type=</span><span class="st">&quot;audio/mpeg&quot;</span> /&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>              &lt;/<span class="kw">audio</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;glossary-on-screen&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-34"><a href="#cb1-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-35"><a href="#cb1-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            &lt;<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;definition in the language of the page goes here&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-36"><a href="#cb1-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-37"><a href="#cb1-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-38"><a href="#cb1-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-39"><a href="#cb1-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-40"><a href="#cb1-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-41"><a href="#cb1-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="language-of-interface" class="section">

##### 5.2.6.3 Language of Interface<a href="#h.vnjfsc22x4un" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.vnjfsc22x4un" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

There are some assessment programs that use delivery systems that have
the capability to provide candidates with different languages. In those
cases, it may be desirable for candidates to choose their preferred
language for the delivery system interface, which may or may not also be
the language of the content (see Section 5.2.2.1 Item Translation). Use
the "language-of-interface" support with the preferred language declared
in the "lang" attribute using the
<a href="#bib-BCP-47" class="bibref" data-link-type="biblio">[BCP
47]</a> standard to indicate the preferred language for the delivery
system interface.

**Language of Interface PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">language-of-interface</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;de&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="sign-language" class="section">

##### 5.1.2.4 Sign Language<a href="#h.slx3jkewqbvm" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.slx3jkewqbvm" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The provision of sign language support for candidates is somewhat unique
in that the sign language version is commonly both a translation of the
written content and a different mode of access (video in place of
written text). Sign Language content is commonly delivered
simultaneously with the default, written language of the assessment.
This allows for candidates to not only respond to the questions, but to
access the images and text of the default content.

The code below is a PNP for a candidate who needs sign language for
their assessment, and has specifically requested American Sign Language
(ASL) using the three letter
<a href="#bib-BCP-47" class="bibref" data-link-type="biblio">[BCP
47]</a> language tag (line 7).

**Sign Language PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">sign-language</span> <span class="ot">xml:lang=</span><span class="st">&quot;ase&quot;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The support for the candidate in the QTI 3 item example below is
"sign-language" with an ASL language preference ("ase"
<a href="#bib-BCP-47" class="bibref" data-link-type="biblio">[BCP
47]</a> language tag, which is stated in the lang attribute in the
qti-card-entry element (line 42). This example shows how a single video
that applies to the whole item could be referenced from the
qti-item-body element. In this example only one language option is
