#### 5.2.8 Alternative Content Supports<a href="#h.z69nuqkbnl5v" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.z69nuqkbnl5v" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

This section contains supports that provide alternative content that is
intended only for candidates that have specifically requested (via their
PNP) the content. There is some variation how the content is presented
to the candidate, which may be different according to the support.
Alternative content supports can be supplied in addition to the default
content, or completely replace the default content. While there are
sometimes specific personal preferences about adding or replacing the
alternative content, many supports have an assumed default presentation,
which will be detailed in the specific supports where applicable.

<div id="simplified-graphics" class="section">

##### 5.2.8.1 Simplified Graphics<a href="#h.mgxddo74rtyj" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.mgxddo74rtyj" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Some candidates may benefit from a simplified version of a more
complicated graphic. The simplified version of the graphic may remove
extraneous decoration, or in same cases even reduce the amount of
information displayed.

When a candidate is assigned to "simplified-graphics", it indicates that
the candidate should be presented with simplified versions of graphics
when available.

**Simplified Graphics PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">simplified-graphics</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The simplified graphic can be stored within a catalog that supports the
graphic, as shown in the example below. There is some discretion on the
part of the delivery interface as to how to provide the alternative,
either as the default representation without access to the original
graphic, as the default representation where you can swap between the
two versions, or as the alternate to the original version, which the
candidate can swap to at their request.

**Simplified Graphics Alternative Content**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;The image below shows how light reaches the surface of the sun.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">img</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;images/photon-cycle-complex.svg&quot;</span> <span class="ot">alt=</span><span class="st">&quot;A graphic showing the layers of the sun.&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;sun-photon-cycle&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;sun-photon-cycle&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;simplified-graphics&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> <span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;image/svg+xml&quot;</span>&gt;images/photon-cycle-simplified.svg&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="simplified-language-portions" class="section">

##### 5.2.8.2 Simplified Language Portions<a href="#h.m0n45p9laxyn" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.m0n45p9laxyn" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Simplified language (also referred to as "plain language") can be
helpful for some candidates who might struggle comprehending the
intended meaning of the language when more complex words and sentence
structures are used.

Simplified language content is still the language of the item. Programs
may opt to provide simplified language portions for all the languages
available for the assessment, though often only the primary language has
the accommodation.

**Simplified Language Portions PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;<span class="kw">simplified-language-portions</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The simplified language can be stored within a catalog that supports the
graphic, as shown in the example below. There is some discretion on the
part of the delivery interface as to how to provide the alternate
content, either as the default representation without access to the
original content, as the default representation where you can swap
between the two versions of content, or as the alternate to the original
version, which the candidate can swap to at their request.

**Simplified Language Portions Alternative Content**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;cat1&quot;</span>&gt;Read both passages closely, taking note of key points, </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    and looking for differences in order to answer the following question. What was the </span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    difference between the two protagonists? Use details to support your observations.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;cat1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;simplified-language-portions&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;How to answer this question:&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">ul</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;Read the text that applies to this item.&lt;/<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;Make notes about the differences in the main character.&lt;/<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;Write down the differences in your answer.&lt;/<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">ul</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="spoken-prerecorded-or-catalog-pronunciation" class="section">

##### 5.2.8.3 Spoken (prerecorded or catalog pronunciation)<a href="#h.10y5z1rquchz" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.10y5z1rquchz" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

While QTI 3 does provide the capability to use inline pronunciation and
the use of assistive technology to render text-to-speech auditorily (as
an alternative to the default text representation), there are some
implementations that require the delivery of prerecorded audio files to
ensure the pronunciation and quality of the audio rendering. The example
below also includes catalogs that provide pronunciation markup and
storage of audio files.

For this example, a custom support called
"vendorname-textToSpeechPronunciationSSML" is used to store the SSML
pronunciation markup. This is an example of a vendor using a custom
support to use as an interim step, in this case where the SSML markup is
authored, and then the item is processed so that sound files are
generated to multiple audio formats and stored in cards within the same
catalog that had the SSML markup. The intended audience for the custom
support is the vendor themselves (a nifty trick), and the content is not
intended to be presented to the candidate. The intended audience for the
prerecorded sound files is the reading-type computer-read-aloud
candidates, so the files are stored within a qti-card-entry, and a
custom attribute is used to specify that the sound files are only for
the reading-type "computer-read-aloud" candidates. The intent of the
storage of the audio files is to present the audio as an alternative to
the written text provided in the default content.

**Alternative Content for Spoken Content Using Pre-recorded Sound
Files**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0   </span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   identifier=&quot;computer_read_aloud_prerecorded_example&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    title=&quot;In which, SSML is used to generate audio files for delivery.&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    adaptive=&quot;false&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    time-dependent=&quot;false&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xml:lang=&quot;en&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;stem_1&quot;</span>&gt;Canada and the United States share 4 out of the 5 Great </span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>Lakes in central North America.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;prompt_1&quot;</span>&gt;Which lake is entirely within the boundaries of </span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>the United States?&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">p</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;response_1&quot;</span>&gt;Type your answer here: &lt;<span class="kw">qti-text-entry-interaction</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">expected-length=</span><span class="st">&quot;15&quot;</span> <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>/&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;stem_1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;ext:vendorname-textToSpeechPronunciationSSML&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">speak</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Canada and the &lt;<span class="kw">emphasis</span> <span class="ot">level=</span><span class="st">&quot;strong&quot;</span>&gt;United States&lt;/<span class="kw">emphasis</span>&gt; share</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">say-as</span> <span class="ot">interpret-as=</span><span class="st">&quot;cardinal&quot;</span>&gt;4&lt;/<span class="kw">say-as</span>&gt; out of the </span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">say-as</span> <span class="ot">interpret-as=</span><span class="st">&quot;cardinal&quot;</span>&gt;5&lt;/<span class="kw">say-as</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">break</span> <span class="ot">time=</span><span class="st">&quot;250ms&quot;</span>/&gt; Great Lakes in central North America.</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">speak</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;spoken&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-34"><a href="#cb1-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">data-reading-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;computer-read-aloud&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-35"><a href="#cb1-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-36"><a href="#cb1-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;audio/mpeg&quot;</span>&gt;apipv1p0_CoreTest_VC_IP_04_Sound.mp3&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-37"><a href="#cb1-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-38"><a href="#cb1-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;audio/ogg&quot;</span>&gt;apipv1p0_CoreTest_VC_IP_04_Sound.ogg&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-39"><a href="#cb1-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-40"><a href="#cb1-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;application/xml&quot;</span>&gt;apipv1p0_CoreTest_VC_IP_04_Sound.xml&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-41"><a href="#cb1-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-42"><a href="#cb1-42" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-43"><a href="#cb1-43" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-44"><a href="#cb1-44" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;prompt_1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-45"><a href="#cb1-45" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;ext:vendorname-textToSpeechPronunciationSSML&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-46"><a href="#cb1-46" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-47"><a href="#cb1-47" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">speak</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-48"><a href="#cb1-48" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>           &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Which lake is entirely within the boundaries of the United States?&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-49"><a href="#cb1-49" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">speak</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-50"><a href="#cb1-50" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-51"><a href="#cb1-51" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-52"><a href="#cb1-52" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;spoken&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-53"><a href="#cb1-53" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">data-reading-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;computer-read-aloud&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-54"><a href="#cb1-54" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-55"><a href="#cb1-55" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;audio/mpeg&quot;</span>&gt;apipv1p0_CoreTest_VC_IP_04_Sound1.mp3&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-56"><a href="#cb1-56" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-57"><a href="#cb1-57" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;audio/ogg&quot;</span>&gt;apipv1p0_CoreTest_VC_IP_04_Sound1.ogg&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-58"><a href="#cb1-58" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-59"><a href="#cb1-59" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;application/xml&quot;</span>&gt;apipv1p0_CoreTest_VC_IP_04_Sound1.xml&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-60"><a href="#cb1-60" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-61"><a href="#cb1-61" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-62"><a href="#cb1-62" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-63"><a href="#cb1-63" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;response_1&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-64"><a href="#cb1-64" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;ext:vendorname-textToSpeechPronunciationSSML&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-65"><a href="#cb1-65" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-66"><a href="#cb1-66" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">speak</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-67"><a href="#cb1-67" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Type your answer here:&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-68"><a href="#cb1-68" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;/<span class="kw">speak</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-69"><a href="#cb1-69" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-70"><a href="#cb1-70" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-71"><a href="#cb1-71" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;spoken&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-72"><a href="#cb1-72" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">data-reading-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;computer-read-aloud&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-73"><a href="#cb1-73" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-74"><a href="#cb1-74" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;audio/mpeg&quot;</span>&gt;apipv1p0_CoreTest_VC_IP_04_Sound2.mp3&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-75"><a href="#cb1-75" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-76"><a href="#cb1-76" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;audio/ogg&quot;</span>&gt;apipv1p0_CoreTest_VC_IP_04_Sound2.ogg&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-77"><a href="#cb1-77" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-78"><a href="#cb1-78" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;application/xml&quot;</span>&gt;apipv1p0_CoreTest_VC_IP_04_Sound2.xml&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-79"><a href="#cb1-79" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-80"><a href="#cb1-80" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-81"><a href="#cb1-81" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-82"><a href="#cb1-82" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-83"><a href="#cb1-83" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="tactile-file" class="section">

##### 5.2.8.4 Tactile (file)<a href="#h.l8cfa25rl0aw" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.l8cfa25rl0aw" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Some graphics used in assessment items have a level of complexity that
becomes difficult to describe using words. Many assessment programs
provide tactile representations of these complex graphics for candidates
who may benefit from the tactiles.

In the example below, the candidate is assigned to the tactile support.
While it isn't required to include the braille support, they are
supports that are commonly used together. While not all tactile
representations include braille content, some do. For programs that
supply multiple braille versions of the tactile representations,
providing the braille preferences will help ensure the candidate
receives the tactiles with their braille preferences.

**Tactile PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;<span class="kw">tactile</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;<span class="kw">braille</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">braille-type</span>&gt;ubae&lt;/<span class="kw">braille-type</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">grade</span>&gt;2&lt;/<span class="kw">grade</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">math-type</span>&gt;nemeth&lt;/<span class="kw">math-type</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;/<span class="kw">braille</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

While some assessment programs provide tactiles in pre-embossed packets,
other programs allow for tactile files to be printed on a braille
embosser prior or during the assessment session. The tactile files can
be included in a QTI 3 content package, and those files can be
referenced within the item file, as shown in the example below.

**Tactile File Reference in an Item File**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0   </span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   identifier=&quot;ac896786&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    title=&quot;In which, there is supplemental content for candidates with the spoken </span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>support with different reading-types&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    time-dependent=&quot;false&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xml:lang=&quot;en&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;The water cycle ...&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">img</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;graphics/water_cycle.svg&quot;</span> <span class="ot">height=</span><span class="st">&quot;300&quot;</span> <span class="ot">width=</span><span class="st">&quot;400&quot;</span> <span class="ot">alt=</span><span class="st">&quot;Picture of the water </span></span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">  cycle.&quot;</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;thx1138&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;Which part of the water cycle is exothermic?&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Type your answer here: &lt;<span class="kw">qti-text-entry-interaction</span> <span class="ot">expected-length=</span><span class="st">&quot;15&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>/&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;thx1138&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;tactile&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Look for tactile sheet THX-1138 to answer this question.&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">ul</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;The sun and sky are in the upper portion of the tactile.&lt;/<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;The mountains are on the left-hand side of the tactile.&lt;/<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;The earth (soil) are on the lower left-hand side of the tactile.&lt;/<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;The body of water is on the lower right-hand side of the tactile.&lt;/<span class="kw">li</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;/<span class="kw">ul</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-32"><a href="#cb1-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> <span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;image/svg+xml&quot;</span>&gt;tactiles/thx1138.svg&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-33"><a href="#cb1-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-34"><a href="#cb1-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-35"><a href="#cb1-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;long-description&quot;</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;These sorts of complicated </span>
<span id="cb1-36"><a href="#cb1-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>images usually have long descriptions.&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-37"><a href="#cb1-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-38"><a href="#cb1-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-39"><a href="#cb1-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-40"><a href="#cb1-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-41"><a href="#cb1-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

See Section 5.2.3.6 for information on providing tactile tour content
for candidates that are given the tactile support.

If the tactiles did have different braille versions, the differences in
the braille representations can be supplied in attributes within the
qti-card-entry elements. The example below shows a code snippet that
differentiates between UEB and EBAE braille encodings. Other braille
differences could also be expressed within attributes, like
braille-grade or math-type, by adding a "data-" prefix to those
parameter names, then using the standard vocabularies of the parameters.
This example also demonstrates referencing a tactile file for the whole
item, rather than a specific graphic. Either way is allowable, and there
are often good reasons for choosing one way over the other.

**Tactile File References for Different Kinds of Braille**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;thx1137&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Some content...<span class="er">&lt;</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;thx1137&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;tactile&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">data-braille-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;ueb&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> <span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;image/svg+xml&quot;</span>&gt;tactiles/thx1137_ueb.svg&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span> <span class="ot">data-braille-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;ebae&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> <span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;image/svg+xml&quot;</span>&gt;tactiles/thx1137_ebae.svg&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card-entry</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

</div>

<div id="computer-interface-supports" class="section">

#### 5.2.9 Computer Interface Supports<a href="#h.xqt1u0sjal2j" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.xqt1u0sjal2j" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Supports in this section are intended to change either the way something
looks or the way something works for the specific candidate who has
requested the support via their PNP.

<div id="high-contrast" class="section">

##### 5.2.9.1 High Contrast<a href="#h.b55x0ox4hv7m" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.b55x0ox4hv7m" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Some candidates may benefit from graphics or a user interface that uses
only high-contrast renderings of the content. To indicate that a
candidate needs high-contrast versions of the content, use the
high-contrast feature in the candidate's PNP, as shown in the example
below.

**High Contrast PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   &lt;<span class="kw">high-contrast</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

To supply a high-contrast alternative for a graphic within an
qti-assessment-item, use the high-contrast vocabulary in the support
attribute of a card within a catalog, as shown in the example below. The
replaced image would retain the other img attribute information (alt,
height, width). If you wish you completely replace the img content, use
an qti-html-content node and introduce a completely different img object
with its own attributes.

**High-Contrast Alternative Content in an qti-assessment-item File**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xmlns:xsi=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">xsi:schemaLocation=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsqtiasi_v3p0   </span></span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqti_asiv3p0p1_v1p0.xsd&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   identifier=&quot;ac896747&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    title=&quot;In which, there is a high-contrast alternative for a graphic.&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    time-dependent=&quot;false&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xml:lang=&quot;en&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-10"><a href="#cb1-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-11"><a href="#cb1-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;The water cycle ...&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-12"><a href="#cb1-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">img</span> <span class="ot">src=</span><span class="st">&quot;graphics/water_cycle.svg&quot;</span> <span class="ot">height=</span><span class="st">&quot;300&quot;</span> <span class="ot">width=</span><span class="st">&quot;400&quot;</span> <span class="ot">alt=</span><span class="st">&quot;Picture of the water </span></span>
<span id="cb1-13"><a href="#cb1-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="st">      cycle.&quot;</span> <span class="ot">data-catalog-idref=</span><span class="st">&quot;b747&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-14"><a href="#cb1-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;Which part of the water cycle is exothermic?&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-15"><a href="#cb1-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    &lt;<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;Type your answer here: &lt;<span class="kw">qti-text-entry-interaction</span> <span class="ot">expected-length=</span><span class="st">&quot;15&quot;</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-16"><a href="#cb1-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="ot">response-identifier=</span><span class="st">&quot;RESPONSE&quot;</span>/&gt;&lt;/<span class="kw">p</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-17"><a href="#cb1-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-18"><a href="#cb1-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-19"><a href="#cb1-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-20"><a href="#cb1-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;b747&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-21"><a href="#cb1-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;high-contrast&quot;</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-22"><a href="#cb1-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        &lt;<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span> <span class="ot">mime-type=</span><span class="st">&quot;image/svg+xml&quot;</span>&gt;graphics/thx1138_hc.svg&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-file-href</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-23"><a href="#cb1-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-24"><a href="#cb1-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-25"><a href="#cb1-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-card</span> <span class="ot">support=</span><span class="st">&quot;long-description&quot;</span>&gt;&lt;<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;These sorts of complicated </span>
<span id="cb1-26"><a href="#cb1-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>images usually have long descriptions. Put description here...&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-html-content</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-27"><a href="#cb1-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-card</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-28"><a href="#cb1-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>     &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-29"><a href="#cb1-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-30"><a href="#cb1-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> &lt;/<span class="kw">qti-catalog-info</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-31"><a href="#cb1-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="input-requirements-specific-to-the-candidate" class="section">

##### 5.2.9.2 Input Requirements Specific to the Candidate<a href="#h.pgnl267ej42a" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.pgnl267ej42a" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Using the input-requirement supports in a candidate PNP allow you to
providethe requirements for the user to fully operate a computer
interface using only their preferred input method (i.e., keyboard,
mouse, etc.).

**PNP**: The term "input-requirements" indicates a user's computer input
requirements.

There are four possible options for input-requirements, including:

1.  full-keyboard-control
2.  full-mouse-control
3.  full-switch-control
4.  full-touchscreen-control

For each of the input-requirement values, they do not mean that the user
cannot use other methods of input (the interface should not prevent
these other methods), but that the interface must have full control of
the interface using the method provided in their PNP.

**Input Requirements PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">input-requirements</span>&gt;full-keyboard-control&lt;/<span class="kw">input-requirements</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="keyword-emphasis" class="section">

##### 5.2.9.3 Keyword Emphasis<a href="#h.6jafomq67v0j" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.6jafomq67v0j" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Some candidates might benefit from having key (cognitively important)
words in the content brought to their attention, to help them better
understand the meaning of the content. In QTI 3, you would use the QTI 3
specific class "qti-keyword-emphasis" on the element that you would like
to have emphasis. The emphasis is intended to be limited to those
candidates who have been assigned the keyword-emphasis support in their
PNP.

**Keyword Emphasis in a Candidate PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">keyword-emphasis</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

**Keyword Emphasis in an qti-assessment-item**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">qti-prompt</span> <span class="ot">id=</span><span class="st">&quot;prompt1&quot;</span>&gt;Indicate which of the following statements are &lt;<span class="kw">span</span> </span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="ot">class=</span><span class="st">&quot;qti-keyword-emphasis&quot;</span>&gt;accurate&lt;/<span class="kw">span</span>&gt;.&lt;/<span class="kw">prompt</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>...</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-item-body</span>&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">qti-assessment-item</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

While there is no specific recommended approach as to how to emphasize
words, the expectation is that the words within the
"qti-keyword-emphasis" class would appear to be different, with greater
emphasis than the surrounding text, and that the emphasis would only be
presented to those candidates who are intended to see this extra
emphasis for special words. For words intended to be emphasized for all
candidates, use more traditional HTML techniques to indicate the
emphasis (strong, em, etc.).

</div>

<div id="answer-masking" class="section">

##### 5.2.9.4 Answer Masking<a href="#h.zfsilwvs9kun" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.zfsilwvs9kun" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Some Candidates may benefit from considering the answer of a question
before they consider the possible answer choices.

In QTI 3, use the "answer-masking" support in a candidate's PNP to give
the candidate the option to hide the answer choices when they initially
are presented items with choice interactions. To indicate that a
candidate needs answer-masking, use the answer-masking support in the
candidate's PNP, as shown in the example below. Note that some
assessment programs allow this tool as an option for all test
candidates.

**Answer Masking in a Candidate PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">answer-masking</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

For the answer-masking tool to be effective, the choices *MUST* be
wholly visually and programmatically unavailable when the candidate is
presented the item. For candidates who are assigned the answer-masking
support, the answer-masking tool *MUST* be turned on by default for the
candidate as they begin their testing session (these mask(s) are only
required for choice interactions).

The candidate *MUST* be made aware that a mask is in place, both through
visual indicators and programmatic markup (content for assistive
technology). The candidate *MUST* be able to remove the mask at a time
of their choosing.

Answer-masking may be provided either as a single mask covering all the
responses or as individual masks that cover each response. There is no
requirement for delivery systems that after responses have been
"revealed" to the candidate that the responses can be re-masked by the
candidate. Additionally, when returning to an item where the candidate
has already unmasked response options, the delivery system is not
required (or expected) to restore the masks.

</div>

<div id="layout-single-column" class="section">

##### 5.12.9.5 Layout Single Column<a href="#h.qmc14wpspb8m" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.qmc14wpspb8m" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Some candidates benefit from content being presented in a single column
rather than multi-column layouts or side-by-side windows. This request
is commonly made by users with low-vision to prevent horizontal
scrolling, which adds time and cognitive load to comprehension.

Delivery systems may need to reflow text content to achieve a single
column layout. Delivery systems should still be mindful of the character
widths of text blocks (paragraphs) but may need to have wider columns
for the presentation of graphics or tables of data.

Delivery systems may have circumstances where adjusting the layout into
single columns is not possible, or it may hinder the measurement of
testing construct. In these cases, the content does not need to be
presented to candidates in a single column.

Use the layout-single-column support is a candidates PNP to indicate
they wish to view assessment content in a single column.

**Layout Single Column in a Candidate PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">layout-single-column</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</div>

<div id="magnification" class="section">

##### 5.2.9.6 Magnification<a href="#h.mxshveu582ix" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.mxshveu582ix" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The ability to magnify content is a common support provided to all
candidates, and meets the WCAG 2.0 Success Criteria 1.4.4 Resize Text
which specifies that text needs to be resizable up to 200 percent
without the need for assistive technology.

Some candidates have magnification needs greater than 200 percent, or
may wish to be specific that they only want text to be magnified, and to
the extent possible, keep the layout of the page (with the exception of
text that is reflowed). QTI 3 implementers may wish to read the WCAG 2.1
Success Criteria 1.4.10 Reflow for more guidance on how to meet the
needs of users with magnification needs.

The QTI 3 standard has three ways (magnification types) to indicate a
candidate's preference for the "magnification" support, namely "text",
"non-text", and "all-content". In either case, an optional, specific
amount of magnification can be indicated as a number in the
"zoom-amount" attribute in the respective options. Candidates *MUST*
have the option to change the amount of magnification (at least up to
400 percent) at any time during an assessment session. If the PNP
supplies a specific zoom-amount in their preference, that amount should
be the amount of magnification when the candidate begins their
assessment session.

If a candidate is not assigned to a specific type of magnification, a
general magnification mechanism *MUST* be provided for the candidate
(the all-content magnification type), where at least the text is
magnified up to 400 percent.

Below are descriptions of the three types of magnification:

<div id="text" class="section">

###### 5.2.9.6.1 Text<a href="#h.1vceihka6c8k" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.1vceihka6c8k" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

The text within the "main content" of the assessment is capable of being
magnified up to 400%. The main content is considered the item text
content (including text within interactions), stimuli, candidate-facing
rubric block content, and feedback content.

In this setting, the text blocks are expected to reflow within their
containers, maintaining their horizontal widths. Delivery systems have
the discretion to determine when maintaining the container widths are no
longer sustainable and may alter the layout when they have exceeded the
limits they have defined.

In this setting, graphics are not expected to magnified, nor the text
within the graphics, as enlarged text may change the location of labels
or information as they associated with parts of the graphic.

Text within other areas of the test interface (not part of the main
content, such as buttons and menus) does not need magnification (though
delivery systems may choose to do so).

</div>

<div id="non-text" class="section">

###### 5.2.9.6.2 Non-text<a href="#h.myhntvg3s16l" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.myhntvg3s16l" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

For this type of magnification, all images within the main content (see
above for a description of "main content") are expected to be able to be
magnified up to 400%. Any text within these images *MUST* also be
magnified at the same proportional magnification.

Magnification of other aspects of the test interface is not required or
expected.

</div>

<div id="all-content" class="section">

###### 5.2.9.6.3 All-content<a href="#h.bqx0dloz11xc" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.bqx0dloz11xc" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

For this type of magnification, the entire test interface is expected to
be able to be magnified up to 400%. Candidates may need to use scrolling
to see portions of the test content either through horizontal or
vertical scrolling.

The delivery system may use their discretion regarding the use of
responsive design and reflow of text to meet this need and to reduce the
need for horizontal scrolling.

</div>

</div>

<div id="text-appearance" class="section">

##### 5.2.9.7 Text Appearance<a href="#h.1cvvzglvmd93" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#h.1cvvzglvmd93" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Many candidates benefit from the ability to change the appearance of
test content text. The reasons vary from familiarity to perceptibility.
While a PNP might indicate a candidate's general need to change the text
appearance, a PNP may also contain specific text appearance preferences.
The QTI 3 Personal Needs and Preferences profile allows for the
following preferences:

- color-theme
- font-size
- font-face
- line-spacing
- line-height
- letter-spacing
- uniform-font-sizing
- word-spacing
- word-wrapping
- font-color
- background-color

Below are the descriptions of each of the above preferences. If you
wished to indicate generally that a candidate would like to make text
appearance changes, use the text-appearance support in the candidate's
PNP without adding any of the preferences.

**Text Appearance in a Candidate PNP**

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td class="qti_code_example"><div class="example">
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb1" aria-busy="false"><pre
class="sourceCode hljs xml"><code class="sourceCode xml"><span id="cb1-1"><a href="#cb1-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">&lt;?xml</span> <span class="ot">version=</span><span class="st">&quot;1.0&quot;</span> <span class="ot">encoding=</span><span class="st">&quot;UTF-8&quot;</span><span class="fu">?&gt;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-2"><a href="#cb1-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span> <span class="ot">xmlns=</span><span class="st">&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb1-3"><a href="#cb1-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="er"> </span>   xmlns:xsi=&quot;http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance&quot;</span>
<span id="cb1-4"><a href="#cb1-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    xsi:schemaLocation=&quot;http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/qtiv3p0/imsafa3p0pnp_v1p0 </span>
<span id="cb1-5"><a href="#cb1-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/qti/v3p0/schema/xsd/imsqtiv3p0_afa3p0pnp_v1p0.xsd&quot;&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-6"><a href="#cb1-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-7"><a href="#cb1-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  &lt;<span class="kw">text-appearance</span>/&gt;</span>
<span id="cb1-8"><a href="#cb1-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb1-9"><a href="#cb1-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>&lt;/<span class="kw">access-for-all-pnp</span>&gt;</span></code></pre></div>
</div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<div id="color-theme-0" class="section">

###### 5.2.9.7.1 color-theme<a href="#color-theme" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a><a href="#color-theme" class="self-link" aria-label="§"></a>

Color-theme allows a candidate to specify their preferred foreground
(text) color and background color combination for the presentation of
assessment content.

If a candidate's PNP contains the color-theme support, they *MUST* be
able to change the color-theme at any time after logging into the test
session.

Additionally, the candidate *MUST* be able to return the default
interface color at any time during the testing session. Images and
graphics can be negatively impacted by interface color choices, enabling
the candidate to return to the default rendering where the candidate can
view the images as originally authored. To help mitigate possible color
conflicts, the recommended practice is to use opaque backgrounds for
graphics, including adding a white background to SVG formatted images.

Within a PNP, a candidate can request the ability to request their
preferred color-theme by stating:

``` hljs
<text-appearance>
            <color-theme />
          </text-appearance>

```

Alternatively, if an assessment program wishes to make color choices
available for all candidates, the delivery system can give the candidate
the ability to choose from color-themes (often supplied as a series of
swatches).

Color-theme uses specific vocabulary to guide delivery systems for a
predictable color-preference of the candidate. By convention, the
enumerated list below states either a conceptual theme or a pair of
colors, where the first color is intended to convey the foreground or
text/font color, and the second color is intended to convey the overall
background color. The term "reversed" means to use the opposite hue and
value of the non-reversed theme.

The full color-theme vocabulary:

- yellow-blue
- blue-yellow
- black-rose
- rose-black
- black-cyan
- cyan-black
- black-cream
- cream-black
- medgray-darkgray
- darkgray-medgray
- default
- default-reverse
- high-contrast
- high-contrast-reverse
- ext:\[string, 256 character limit\] (an extension point for
  program-specific color themes)

The enumerated sample colors provided below are for guidance only, and
not required to be followed exactly. However, the delivery system is
expected to stay within the color family requested (when using named
color pair themes) and *MUST* meet the contrast requirements for the
color-theme. The delivery system determines which exact colors are used
for a named theme, and which colors to use as secondary colors for the
other various aspects of the user interface (links, highlights, buttons,
etc.) for any given named color-theme.

For delivery systems providing a choice of presentation color themes, be
aware there are other interface elements beyond the text and background
colors which will be affected by the change in colors, including buttons
(including the various interaction states), highlighting, text
selection, etc. The recommendation is to make interface changes
appropriate to the colors used and the contrast guidelines for that
combination.

The example below is the "default" color-theme of a delivery system,
with a menu of possible color-theme choices offered to the candidate.
Screenshots are provided courtesy of amp-up.io on their open-source QTI
3 Player ©2022.

<img
src="/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl5_defaultColorTheme.png"
width="624" height="360"
alt="Test Runner interface showing the default color theme." />

The screenshot below is an example of a candidate choosing an
alternative color-theme offered in the delivery system.

<img
src="/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl5_altColorTheme.png"
width="624" height="360"
alt="Test Runner interface showing the yellow-blue color theme." />



**color-theme: yellow-blue**

Yellow text on a dark-blue (navy) background. This color-theme is
intended to give the candidate a high-contrast of foreground and
background in both hue and value.

Sample text colors: yellow

- \#FFCC00
- \#FBEB5B

![swatch for yellow
FFCC00](/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl5_FFCC00.png)![swatch
for yellow
FBEB5B](/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl5_FBEB5B.png)

Sample background colors: blue

- \#003398
- \#000080 (navy)

![swatch for blue
003398](/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl5_003398.png)<img src="/sites/default/files/specs/images/qti/3p0/impl5_000080.png"
width="216" height="170" alt="swatch for blue 000080" />

Contrast Guidelines:

- The contrast *MUST* be at least 7:1 between the text and background
  colors (meets WCAG acceptance criteria 1.4.6 for Contrast Minimum).
- It is recommended to also follow the WCAG acceptance criteria 1.4.11
  for Non-Text Contrast (contrast ratio of 3:1) for the remaining color
  choices.



**color-theme: blue-yellow**

Dark blue (navy) text on a yellow background. The blue-yellow theme
reverses the foreground and background colors of the yellow-blue theme
where the text is darker than the background.

See the above yellow-blue color-theme for sample colors and contrast
requirements.



**color-theme: black-rose**

Black text on pinkish colored background. The black-rose theme imitates
